subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge (IRK)
q:
'By Him who dominates my soul I swear that you are the Prophet of this nation . . . you will be abused and you will be persecuted. If I should ever live to see that day, I shal surely help the cause of Allah.'
Who made the statement above?
explanation: The statement was made by Waraqah b. Nawfal. He was a cousin of Khadijah (RA), Prophet Muhammad's (SAW) first wife. He was a Christian monk who had knowledge of the previous scriptures and had predicted the coming of a prophet in Arabia. When Prophet Muhammad (SAW) received his first revelation, Khadijah (RA) took him to Waraqah who confirmed his prophethood.

topics.title: THE LIFE OF PROPHET MUHAMMAD (SAW) : HIS CALL TO PROPHETHOOD

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge (IRK)
q: The ninth year of hijrah is known as the year of
explanation: The ninth year of hijrah is known as the year of Deputation. This is because it was in this year that various tribes and communities sent their representatives to Madinah to learn about Islam and the Prophet Muhammad (SAW). These deputations resulted in many conversions to Islam.

topics.title: THE LIFE OF PROPHET MUHAMMAD (SAW) : THE HIJRAH

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge (IRK)
q: After the Prophet (S.A.W.) had migrated to al-Madinah, the first two major wars he fought against the Makkans were
explanation: After the Prophet (S.A.W.) had migrated to al-Madinah, the first two major wars he fought against the Makkans were Badr and Uhud. The Battle of Badr was the first major battle, which took place in the second year of Hijrah. The Battle of Uhud was the second major battle, which took place in the third year of Hijrah.

topics.title: THE LIFE OF PROPHET MUHAMMAD (SAW) : THE BATTLES OF BADR, UHUD AND KHANDAQ: CAUSES AND EFFECTS

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge (IRK)
q: The Prophet's uncle who lost his life in the battle of Badr was
explanation: The Prophet's uncle who lost his life in the battle of Badr was Hamzah. Hamzah was a staunch supporter of Prophet Muhammad (SAW) and was known for his bravery. He was martyred in the Battle of Uhud, not Badr, by Wahshi ibn Harb, a slave who was promised freedom by Jubayr ibn Mut'im if he killed Hamzah.

topics.title: THE LIFE OF PROPHET MUHAMMAD (SAW) : THE BATTLES OF BADR, UHUD AND KHANDAQ: CAUSES AND EFFECTS

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge (IRK)
q: The Quraysh boycotted Banu Hashim because they
options: planned to migrate to Yathrib with the Prophet (S.A.W.)
protected the Prophet (S.A.W.) and refused to surrender him
explanation: The Quraysh boycotted Banu Hashim because they protected the Prophet (S.A.W.) and refused to surrender him. This boycott was a social and economic embargo against the Muslims and the clan of Banu Hashim, who were mostly Muslims. The boycott lasted for three years and was lifted when the document enforcing the boycott was found to be eaten by termites except for the name of Allah.

topics.title: THE LIFE OF PROPHET MUHAMMAD (SAW) : HIS CALL TO PROPHETHOOD

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge (IRK)
q: The success of the business venture which Muhammad (S.A.W.) carried out for Khadijah was due mainly to
options: the fact that he was a prophet
the skill and hard work of his assistants
explanation: The success of the business venture which Muhammad (S.A.W.) carried out for Khadijah was due mainly to his dedication and honesty. Muhammad (S.A.W.) was known for his honesty and integrity, which earned him the nickname Al-Amin, meaning 'the trustworthy'. His honesty in business dealings attracted more customers and brought success to the business.

topics.title: THE LIFE OF PROPHET MUHAMMAD (SAW) : HIS CALL TO PROPHETHOOD

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge (IRK)
q: The social reforms witnessed by Arabia during the life time of the Prophet (S.A.W.) included the
options: universal recognition of the Arabs as the superior race
explanation:
improvement in slave/master relations.

While slavery was not completely abolished during the Prophet's time, Islam significantly improved the treatment of slaves, promoting humane and just relations between slaves and their masters, encouraging the freeing of slaves, and establishing rights for them.

topics.title: ISLAMIC HISTORY AND CIVILIZATION - PRE-ISLAMIC ARABIA JAHILIYYAH : ISLAMIC REFORMS

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge (IRK)
q: The treaty of al-Hudaybiyyah was expected to last
explanation: The treaty of al-Hudaybiyyah was expected to last ten years. This treaty was signed between the Prophet Muhammad (S.A.W.) and the Quraysh of Mecca in 628 AD. The terms of the treaty included a ten-year truce and the agreement that Muslims could perform the pilgrimage the following year.

topics.title: THE LIFE OF PROPHET MUHAMMAD (SAW) : THE TREATY OF AL-HUDAIBIYYAH AND THE CONQUEST OF MAKKAH

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge (IRK)
q: The Khulafa al-Rashidun reigned between
explanation: The Khulafa al-Rashidun, also known as the Rightly Guided Caliphs, reigned between A.D. 632 and 661. This period started with the caliphate of Abu Bakr after the death of Prophet Muhammad (S.A.W.) in 632 AD and ended with the assassination of Ali, the fourth caliph, in 661 AD.

topics.title: THE RIGHTLY GUIDED CALIPHS AL-KHULAFA'U RASHIDUN : THE LIVES AND CONTRIBUTIONS OF THE FOUR RIGHTLY GUIDED CALIPHS

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge (IRK)
q: The conversion of 'Umar b. al-Khattab was a turning point in the history of Islam because
options: it encouraged the actualization of the Prophet's mission
the entire Quraysh were immediately converted to Islam
he publicly announced his migration to al-Madinah and was not challenged
the boycott of Banu Hashim was terminated
explanation: The conversion of 'Umar b. al-Khattab was a turning point in the history of Islam because it encouraged the actualization of the Prophet's mission. 'Umar was a strong and influential man in Mecca, and his conversion to Islam gave the Muslim community more confidence and strength. His conversion also led to an increase in the number of people accepting Islam.

topics.title: THE LIFE OF PROPHET MUHAMMAD (SAW) : HIS CALL TO PROPHETHOOD

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge (IRK)
q: The battle of Siffin led to the emergence of the
explanation: The Battle of Siffin was a significant event in Islamic history that led to the emergence of the Khawarij. The Khawarij were a group of extremists who seceded from the main body of Muslims due to their disagreement with the arbitration process that was agreed upon to resolve the conflict between Caliph Ali and Muawiyah, the governor of Syria. They believed in the absolute authority of the Quran and rejected any form of human arbitration.

topics.title: THE LIFE OF PROPHET MUHAMMAD (SAW) : THE BATTLES OF BADR, UHUD AND KHANDAQ: CAUSES AND EFFECTS

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge (IRK)
q: During the reign of Caliph 'Ali, the seat of administration moved from al-Madinah to
explanation: During the reign of Caliph 'Ali, the seat of administration moved from al-Madinah to Kufah. Kufah was a city in present-day Iraq. Ali moved his capital to Kufah in order to have a strategic advantage during the civil war that was taking place during his caliphate.

topics.title: THE RIGHTLY GUIDED CALIPHS AL-KHULAFA'U RASHIDUN : THE LIVES AND CONTRIBUTIONS OF THE FOUR RIGHTLY GUIDED CALIPHS

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge (IRK)
q: The first Quranic verse revealed, instructed the Prophet (S.A.W.) to
explanation: The first Quranic verse revealed was 'Read in the name of your Lord who created' (Surah Al-Alaq, 96:1). This verse instructed the Prophet Muhammad (S.A.W.) to seek knowledge through reading. This marked the beginning of the revelation of the Quran which continued for 23 years until the death of the Prophet.

topics.title: THE QUR’AN AND HADITH – REVELATION OF THE GLORIOUS QUR’AN : HIS REACTION TO THE FIRST REVELATION AND ITS IMPORTANCE

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge (IRK)
q: The surah that tells us the story of how the Ka'abah was delivered from destruction is
explanation: The Surah that tells us the story of how the Ka'abah was delivered from destruction is Surah al-Fil. This Surah narrates the incident of the 'People of the Elephant' who intended to destroy the Ka'abah but were defeated and their plan was thwarted by birds sent by Allah who dropped stones on them.

topics.title: THE QUR'AN AND HADITH - REVELATION OF THE GLORIOUS QUR'AN : DIFFERENT MODES OF REVELATION: INSPIRATION BEHIND THE VEIL, THROUGH AN ANGEL, ETC.

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge (IRK)
q: In Surat an-Nas, protection was sought from the evils of
explanation: In Surat an-Nas, protection is sought from the evils of men and jinn. The Surah is a prayer for protection from the evil whispers and suggestions of Satan, who is described as whispering into the hearts of men and jinn. It is the last Surah of the Quran and is often recited for protection from evil.

topics.title: ARTICLE OF FAITH : BELIEF IN ALLAH

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge (IRK)
q: Refence to a special night is made in Surat
explanation: Surat al-Qadr refers to the special night, which is Laylat al-Qadr, the Night of Decree. This night is considered the holiest night of the year for Muslims, and is traditionally celebrated on the 27th day of Ramadan. It is known as the night when the first verses of the Quran were revealed to the Prophet Muhammad.

topics.title: STUDY OF THE ARABIC TEXT OF THE FOLLOWING SUWAR/AYATS WITH TAJWID : AL-QADR

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge (IRK)
q: The verse Wa la sawfa yu tika rabbuka fatarda is immediately preceded by
options: thumma latus alunna yawma idhin anin-na im
explanation:
The verse "Wa la sawfa yuʿṭīka rabbuka fa-tarḍā" (Qur'an 93:5) is immediately preceded by:

"Wal-ākhiratu khayrun laka mina l-ūlā" (Qur'an 93:4)

topics.title: STUDY OF THE ARABIC TEXT OF THE FOLLOWING SUWAR/AYATS WITH TAJWID : AD-DUHA

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge (IRK)
q: A Makkan surah with the main objective of reassuring the Prophet (S.A.W.) at a time of distress is
explanation: Surah Ad-Duha was revealed in Makkah and it is a Makkan Surah. The main objective of this Surah was to reassure the Prophet (S.A.W.) at a time of distress and grief due to the pause in revelation. The Surah reassures the Prophet that Allah has not abandoned him.

topics.title: STUDY OF THE ARABIC TEXT OF THE FOLLOWING SUWAR/AYATS WITH TAJWID : AD-DUHA

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge (IRK)
q: The verse (Q. 17. 23) warns against
explanation: The verse Qur'an 17:23 warns against being disrespectful or disobedient to one's parents by insulting them.

topics.title: MORAL LESSONS IN THE GLORIOUS QUR'AN AND HADITH : GOODNESS TO PARENTS

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge (IRK)
q: Suratul-Kawthar concerns the Prophet's
explanation: Suratul-Kawthar concerns the Prophet's loss of his son. The Surah was revealed after the death of the Prophet's son, Abdullah. The disbelievers of Makkah rejoiced at this personal loss of the Prophet, and this Surah was revealed as a consolation to the Prophet, assuring him that he indeed had been granted 'Al-Kawthar' (abundance of good).

topics.title: STUDY OF THE ARABIC TEXT OF THE FOLLOWING SUWAR/AYATS WITH TAJWID : AL -KAWTHAR

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge (IRK)
q: In the verse Ya ayyuhal -ladhina amanu la taqrabus-salata wa antum sukara (Q. 4. 43) Allah is asking the Muslims not to attempt to
explanation: In the verse 'Ya ayyuhal -ladhina amanu la taqrabus-salata wa antum sukara' (Q. 4. 43), Allah is asking the Muslims not to attempt to pray when they are intoxicated. The verse translates to 'O you who have believed, do not approach prayer while you are intoxicated until you know what you are saying.' This verse was revealed to ensure that Muslims are in a clear state of mind during their prayers.

topics.title: IBADAT AND THEIR TYPES : SALAH

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge (IRK)
q: The verse (Q. 95.8) means 'Is Allah not'
explanation:
Qur'an 95:8 (Surah At-Tin, verse 8) says:

"Alaysa Allahu bi-ahkamil ḥākimīn?"
"Is not Allah the best of judges?"

It is a rhetorical question.

topics.title: STUDY OF THE ARABIC TEXT OF THE FOLLOWING SUWAR/AYATS WITH TAJWID : AT-TIN

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge (IRK)
q: The official compilation of Hadith was initiated by
explanation: The official compilation of Hadith was initiated by 'Umar b. Abd al-Aziz. He was the first caliph to order the collection of Hadiths, which were the sayings and actions of Prophet Muhammad.

topics.title: HADITH : HISTORY OF HADITH LITERATURE -COLLECTION OF HADITH FROM THE TIME OF THE PROPHET-SAW TO THE PERIOD OF THE SIX AUTHENTIC COLLECTORS OF HADITH

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge (IRK)
q: The difference between isnad and matn is that
options: matn is the chain of reporters, while isnad is their quality
isnad is the chain of reporters while matn is the text
isnad refers to the reliability of the reporters while matn refers to their weaknesses
explanation: Isnad and Matn are two components of a Hadith. Isnad is the chain of reporters or narrators who transmitted the Hadith from the Prophet Muhammad to the collector of the Hadith. Matn, on the other hand, is the actual text of the Hadith or the content of the message.

topics.title: HADITH : AUTHENTICATION OF HADITH

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge (IRK)
q: The Hadith ". . . " teaches that a Muslim
options: must obey the commands of the Prophet (S.A.W.)
must confine himself to the established teachings of Islam
can contribute in developing and increasing the teachings of Islam
explanation: The Hadith teaches that a Muslim must confine himself to the established teachings of Islam.

topics.title: HADITH : THE RELATIONSHIP BETWEEN HADITH AND THE GLORIOUS QUR’AN

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge (IRK)
q: The expression 'Man kana yu minu billahi wal yawmil akhir fal yaqul khayran aw li yasmut' means a believer should
options: desire for his neighbour what he wishes for himself
explanation: The expression 'Man kana yu minu billahi wal yawmil akhir fal yaqul khayran aw li yasmut' translates to 'Whoever believes in Allah and the Last Day should speak good or keep silent'. This Hadith emphasizes the importance of controlling one's speech and only speaking what is beneficial.

topics.title: HADITH : THE STUDY OF THE ARABIC TEXTS OF THE FOLLOWING AHADITH FROMAN-NAWAWI’S COLLECTION: 1,3,5,6,7,9,10,11,12,13,15,16, 18,19,21,22,25,27,34, AND 41

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge (IRK)
q:
'Verily Allah has prescribed the best behaviour with regard to everything...'
The above quotation from Hadith 17 of an-Nawawi's collection emphasizes the ethics of
explanation: The quotation from Hadith 17 of an-Nawawi's collection emphasizes the ethics of slaughtering animals. It teaches that when one slaughters an animal, they should do it in the most humane way.

topics.title: HADITH : THE STUDY OF THE ARABIC TEXTS OF THE FOLLOWING AHADITH FROMAN-NAWAWI’S COLLECTION: 1,3,5,6,7,9,10,11,12,13,15,16, 18,19,21,22,25,27,34, AND 41

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge (IRK)
q: Which of the following does NOT belong to the group?
explanation: Muslim b. al-Hajjaj does not belong to the group. The other three, Abdullah b. 'Umar, Abdullah b. Mas ud, and Umar b. al-Khattab, were companions of the Prophet Muhammad. Muslim b. al-Hajjaj, however, was a later Islamic scholar known for his collection of Hadith, Sahih Muslim.

topics.title: HADITH : THE SIX SOUND COLLECTORS OF HADITH – BIOGRAPHIES AND THEIR WORKS.

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge (IRK)
q: The Prophet described as ruhullah in the Qur an is
explanation: In the Qur'an, Prophet Isa (Jesus) is referred to as 'Ruhullah', which means 'Spirit of God'. Therefore, the correct answer is Isa.

topics.title: ARTICLE OF FAITH : HIS PROPHETS: ULUL-AZMI

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge (IRK)
q: The above extract from Hadith No. 1 of an- Nawawi is followed by
explanation:
Hadith No. 1 of An-Nawawi's 40 Hadith is the famous narration on intentions:

"Innamal a'maalu bin-niyyaat..."
"Actions are judged by intentions..."

topics.title: HADITH : THE STUDY OF THE ARABIC TEXTS OF THE FOLLOWING AHADITH FROMAN-NAWAWI’S COLLECTION: 1,3,5,6,7,9,10,11,12,13,15,16, 18,19,21,22,25,27,34, AND 41

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge (IRK)
q: The main function of angel Azra il is
explanation: In Islamic tradition, Angel Azra'il is known as the Angel of Death, whose main function is to take the souls of people at the time of death. Therefore, the correct answer is 'taking the lives of people'.

topics.title: ARTICLE OF FAITH : BELIEF IN ALLAH'S ANGELS

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge (IRK)
q: Prophet Isa spoke in the cradle and
explanation: According to the Qur'an, when Prophet Isa (Jesus) was in the cradle, he spoke to protect his mother, Maryam, from the accusations of the people. Therefore, the correct answer is 'exonerated his mother'.

topics.title: ARTICLE OF FAITH : HIS PROPHETS: ULUL-AZMI

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge (IRK)
q: The five most important prophets in Islam were
explanation: The five most important prophets in Islam, known as 'Ulul Azm', are Prophet Muhammad, Nuh (Noah), Ibrahim (Abraham), Musa (Moses), and Isa (Jesus). Therefore, the correct answer is 'Muhammad, Nuh, Ibrahim, Musa, Isa'.

topics.title: ARTICLE OF FAITH : HIS PROPHETS: ULUL-AZMI

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge (IRK)
q: Which of the following Prophets, according to the Quran, lived for not less than nine hundred and fifty years?
explanation: According to the Qur'an, Prophet Nuh (Noah) lived for 950 years. Therefore, the correct answer is 'Nuh'.

topics.title: ARTICLE OF FAITH : HIS PROPHETS: ULUL-AZMI

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge (IRK)
q: Which of the following Prophets was an ancestor of both Arabs and Jews?
explanation: Prophet Ibrahim (Abraham) is considered an ancestor of both Arabs and Jews. According to Islamic tradition, his first son, Ismail (Ishmael), is the ancestor of the Arab people, while his second son, Ishaq (Isaac), is the ancestor of the Jewish people.

topics.title: ARTICLE OF FAITH : BELIEF IN ALLAH'S PROPHETS: ULUL-AZMI

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge (IRK)
q: Iddah, the waiting period after a divorce, is
explanation: Iddah is the waiting period a woman must observe after the death of her spouse or after a divorce, during which she may not marry another man. Its purpose is to ensure that the male parent of any offspring produced after the cessation of a nikah (marriage) would be known. The standard waiting period of Iddah is three menstrual cycles or three months.

topics.title: FAMILY MATTERS : DIVORCE

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge (IRK)
q: Performing Tayammum is justified when one
explanation: Tayammum is a dry ablution in Islamic ritual purification for prayer, which can be used as a substitute for ritual washing (wudu or ghusl) if clean water is not available or if one is sick and unable to use water. It involves the use of clean soil or dust.

topics.title: IBADAT AND THEIR TYPES : TAHARAH, ITS TYPES AND IMPORTANCE AL-ISTINJA'/ISTIJMAR, ALWUDU', AT-TAYAMMUM AND AL-GHUSL HADITH 10 AND 23 OF AN-NAWAWI

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge (IRK)
q: Sawn is the abstinence from eating, drinking and sexual urge
options: from dawn to dusk without intention of worship
from morning to evening in times of distress
with or without the intention of worship
from dawn to dusk with the intention of worship
explanation: Sawn is the practice of fasting, particularly during the month of Ramadan, where Muslims abstain from eating, drinking, and sexual activities from dawn (Fajr) to dusk (Maghrib) with the intention of worship. The intention (niyyah) is a necessary component of the fast.

topics.title: IBADAT AND THEIR TYPES : SAWM

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge (IRK)
q: In order to make Tawaf, you are required to keep the ka abah to your
options: left and go round it in an anticlockwise direction
right and go round it in a clockwise direction
left and go round it in a clockwise direction
right and move round it in an anticlockwise direction
explanation: Tawaf is one of the Islamic rituals of pilgrimage. During the Hajj and Umrah, Muslims are to go around the Ka'bah (the most sacred site in Islam) seven times, in a counterclockwise direction; the first three circuits at a hurried pace on the outer part of the crowd, followed by four times, more closely, at a leisurely pace. The Ka'bah should be on the left side of the person performing Tawaf.

topics.title: IBADAT AND THEIR TYPES : HAJJ

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge (IRK)
q:
The verse
means that divorce may be pronounced twice and then the woman must
options: be retained in honour or allowed to go with kindness
forfeit her wealth and go with kindness
be retained in honour unless she has no children
explanation: Divorce may be pronounced twice and then the woman must be retained in honour or allowed to go with kindness.

topics.title: FAMILY MATTERS : DIVORCE

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge (IRK)
q: One of the conditions of Sunni talaq is that it must be pronounced
options: when the wife is free from menstruation
with the free consent of the wife
explanation: In Sunni Islam, one of the conditions for a valid talaq (divorce) is that the wife must be in a state of purity, i.e., she must be free from menstruation. This is because menstruation is considered a period of physical and emotional discomfort for women, and decisions made during this time may not be clear or rational. Therefore, to ensure fairness and justice, talaq should be pronounced when the wife is free from menstruation.

topics.title: FAMILY MATTERS : DIVORCE

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge (IRK)
q: The first tawaf which a pilgrim makes when he arrives at Makkah is called tawaf
explanation: In the Islamic pilgrimage (Hajj), the first tawaf (circumambulation of the Kaaba) that a pilgrim performs upon arriving in Makkah is called Tawaf al-Qudum. This is an introductory tawaf meant to respect the sanctity of Makkah and the Kaaba.

topics.title: IBADAT AND THEIR TYPES : HAJJ

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge (IRK)
q: If instead of four raka 'at a devotee observes five raka at in salat az-Zuhr he should perform
explanation: If instead of four raka 'at a devotee observes five raka at in salat az-Zuhr he should perform Ba'diyyah.

topics.title: IBADAT AND THEIR TYPES : SALAH

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge (IRK)
q: Having met all the conditions for the payment of Zakah, Mallam Ibrahim has five hundred Niara. How much should he pay as Zakah from this amount?
explanation: The standard percentage of Zakāh (obligatory almsgiving in Islam) is 2.5% (or 1/40) of a Muslim's qualifying wealth that has been held for one lunar year (ḥawl).

topics.title: IBADAT AND THEIR TYPES : ZAKAH

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge (IRK)
q: A worshipper who remembers that he missed an obligatory step of ablution after he had finished praying will have to repeat
explanation: In Islam, ablution (wudu) is a prerequisite for performing the prayer (Salat). If a worshipper remembers that he missed an obligatory step of ablution after he had finished praying, he will have to repeat both the ablution and the prayer. This is because the prayer performed without a proper ablution is not valid.

topics.title: IBADAT AND THEIR TYPES : TAHARAH, ITS TYPES AND IMPORTANCE AL-ISTINJA'/ISTIJMAR, ALWUDU', AT-TAYAMMUM AND AL-GHUSL HADITH 10 AND 23 OF AN-NAWAWI

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge (IRK)
q: The 'Iddah period of a pregnant woman terminates at the
options: end of the third month of delivery
explanation: The 'Iddah period for a pregnant woman in Islam ends at the time of delivery. 'Iddah is a waiting period that a woman must observe after the death of her spouse or after a divorce, during which she may not marry another man. Its purpose is to ensure that the male parent of any offspring produced after the cessation of a nikah (marriage) would be known.

topics.title: FAMILY MATTERS : MARRIAGE

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge (IRK)
q: Of all the special prayers salat al-Janazah is unique because it lacks
explanation: Salat al-Janazah is a special prayer in Islam that is performed at a funeral. It is unique because it lacks ruku (bowing). In this prayer, there is no physical movement of bowing or prostrating, it is mainly a prayer of supplication for the deceased.

topics.title: IBADAT AND THEIR TYPES : SALAH

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge (IRK)
q: Apart from the cleanliness of the body and clothes, the devotee who intends to pray must also ensure the cleanliness of the
explanation: Apart from the cleanliness of the body and clothes, the devotee who intends to pray must also ensure the cleanliness of the place. This is because in Islam, cleanliness is considered half of faith and it is a prerequisite for prayers. The place of prayer must be clean from any impurities.

topics.title: IBADAT AND THEIR TYPES : SALAH

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge (IRK)
q: A father or guardian can give out his daughter or ward in marriage under the principle of Ijbar provided that the
options: girl has attained the age of maturity
groom does not have any physical disability
explanation: Ijbar is an Islamic legal principle that gives a wali (guardian) — typically the father — the authority to contract marriage on behalf of his minor daughter or ward, without her explicit consent.

topics.title: FAMILY MATTERS : MARRIAGE

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge (IRK)
q: Sadaq is the exclusive property of the
explanation: Sadaq is the exclusive property of the wife. Sadaq, also known as Mahr, is a mandatory gift given by the groom to the bride at the time of marriage. It becomes the exclusive property of the wife and she can use it as she pleases.

topics.title: FAMILY MATTERS : MARRIAGE

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge (IRK)
q:
'La yukallifu Hahu nafsan illa wus aha'
This quotation is part of
explanation:
'La yukallifu Hahu nafsan illa wus aha'
This quotation is part of Ayat Amanar- Rasul.

topics.title: STUDY OF THE ARABIC TEXT OF THE FOLLOWING SUWAR/AYATS WITH TAJWID : AL-ALAQ~~STUDY OF THE ARABIC TEXT OF THE FOLLOWING SUWAR/AYATS WITH TAJWID : AL-A'ALA~~STUDY OF THE ARABIC TEXT OF THE FOLLOWING SUWAR/AYATS WITH TAJWID : AYATUL-KURSIY~~STUDY OF THE ARABIC TEXT OF THE FOLLOWING SUWAR/AYATS WITH TAJWID : AMANAR-RASUL

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge (IRK)
q:
Surely, we created man of the best stature. Then we reduced him to the lowest of the low, save those who believe and do good works'.
This quotation is from Surat
explanation: The quotation 'Surely, we created man of the best stature. Then we reduced him to the lowest of the low, save those who believe and do good works' is from Surat al-Tin (The Fig). This Surah discusses the creation of human beings and their ultimate fate.

topics.title: STUDY OF THE ARABIC TEXT OF THE FOLLOWING SUWAR/AYATS WITH TAJWID : AT-TIN

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge (IRK)
q: The memorization, recording and compilation of the Quran ensured its
explanation: The memorization, recording, and compilation of the Quran ensured its authenticity. The Quran was revealed to Prophet Muhammad who memorized it and then it was written down by his companions. This process ensured that the Quran remained authentic and unchanged.

topics.title: PROOF OF THE DIVINE AUTHENTICITY OF THE GLORIOUS QUR'AN : DIVINE PRESERVATION OF THE GLORIOUS QUR'AN

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge (IRK)
q:
'Inna ma yurida ash-shaytanu an yuqi a baynakumul- adawata wal-baghda fil khamr wal-maysir' (Q. 5:91)
The verse quoted above enumerates the evils of
explanation: The verse quoted above enumerates the evils of wine.

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge (IRK)
q: One of the strong proofs of the authenticity of the Quran is that
options: the like of it has not been produced by an individual or a group of people
the Arabic language in which it was revealed is still spoken
it was compiled soon after its revelation
it has been translated into many languages
explanation: One of the strong proofs of the authenticity of the Quran is that the like of it has not been produced by an individual or a group of people. This is a challenge that the Quran itself puts forth in Surah Al-Baqarah (2:23) where it states 'And if you are in doubt about what We have sent down upon Our Servant [Muhammad], then produce a surah the like thereof and call upon your witnesses other than Allah, if you should be truthful.'

topics.title: PROOF OF THE DIVINE AUTHENTICITY OF THE GLORIOUS QUR'AN : UNIQUENESS OF THE GLORIOUS QUR'AN

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge (IRK)
q: In Islam, intoxicants are
explanation: In Islam, intoxicants are forbidden even in small quantity. This is based on several Hadiths (sayings of Prophet Muhammad) and verses from the Quran that prohibit the consumption of intoxicants due to their harmful effects on the individual and society.

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge (IRK)
q:
The Qura'nic verse

(31:18)
teaches against
explanation: The Qura'nic verse 31:18 teaches against arrogance.

q:
'wa lam yakun lahu kufuwan ahad'
This verse of the Quran (112:4) says that Allah
explanation:
Qur'an 112:4 says:

"Wa lam yakun lahu kufuwan aḥad."
"And there is none comparable to Him."

topics.title: TAWHID AND FIQH : KALIMATUSH-SHAHADAH

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge (IRK)
q:
'God was well pleased with the believers when they pledged all allegiance unto thee beneath the tree'.
This verse was revealed in connection with the
explanation: This verse was revealed in connection with the pledge of Aqabah.

topics.title: THE LIFE OF PROPHET MUHAMMAD (SAW) : THE TREATY OF AL-HUDAIBIYYAH AND THE CONQUEST OF MAKKAH

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge (IRK)
q: From which chapter of the Quran is the verse which declares 'Certainly, we created man in the best make'?
explanation: The verse 'Certainly, we created man in the best make' is from Surah At-Tin (The Fig), which is the 95th chapter of the Quran. This verse is a reflection of the Islamic belief in the inherent goodness and dignity of human beings.

topics.title: THE QUR'AN AND HADITH – REVELATION OF THE GLORIOUS QUR’AN

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge (IRK)
q: To maintain the quality of Taqwa one has to be
explanation: Taqwa is an Islamic term for being conscious and cognizant of Allah, of truth, of the rational reality, 'piety, fear of God'. It is often found in the Quran. To maintain the quality of Taqwa, one has to be disciplined. It is not about wealth, poverty, or leadership, but about a person's inner state and their actions.

topics.title: TAWHID AND FIQH : FAITH

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge (IRK)
q: Sahih al-Bukhari is regarded as the most authenti collection of Hadith because its collector was
options: older than any other collector of Hadith
alive during the time of the Prophet (S.A.W.)
a founder of a school of law
explanation: Sahih al-Bukhari is regarded as the most authentic collection of Hadith because its collector, Imam Bukhari, was the most critical of the collectors. He established strict criteria for the acceptance of a Hadith, including the soundness of the chain of narrators and the consistency of the narration with other established facts.

topics.title: HADITH : AUTHENTICATION OF HADITH

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge (IRK)
q: Musnad is a collection of ahadith arranged
options: according to the names of the authorities
explanation: Musnad is a collection of ahadith arranged according to the names of the authorities. This means that the ahadith are grouped by the name of the companion of the Prophet who narrated them. This is different from other collections which may be arranged by topic or other criteria.

topics.title: HADITH : HISTORY OF HADITH LITERATURE -COLLECTION OF HADITH FROM THE TIME OF THE PROPHET-SAW TO THE PERIOD OF THE SIX AUTHENTIC COLLECTORS OF HADITH

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge (IRK)
q: Part of the 23rd Hadith of al-Nawawi's collection refers to purification as a
explanation: The 23rd Hadith of al-Nawawi's collection states that 'Purification is half of faith'. Therefore, purification is referred to as 'half of faith'.

topics.title: HADITH : THE STUDY OF THE ARABIC TEXTS OF THE FOLLOWING AHADITH FROMAN-NAWAWI’S COLLECTION: 1,3,5,6,7,9,10,11,12,13,15,16, 18,19,21,22,25,27,34, AND 41

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge (IRK)
q: Which of the following statements completes the Hadith which says 'He is not a faithful Muslim who eats to his fill while . . .'
options: he is on a journey in the month of Ramadan
his neighbour is hungry beside of him
explanation: The Hadith completes as 'He is not a faithful Muslim who eats to his fill while his neighbour is hungry beside of him'. This Hadith emphasizes the importance of caring for one's neighbours in Islam.

topics.title: MORAL LESSONS IN THE GLORIOUS QUR’AN AND HADITH : DIGNITY OF LABOUR (Q.62:10) (Q.78:11) HADITH FROM BUKHARI AND IBNMAJAH: “THAT ONE OF YOU TAKES HIS ROPE…….” “NEVER HAS ANYONE OF YOU EATEN…..”.

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge (IRK)
q: A Hadith with uninterrupted chain of authorities traced back to the Prophet Muhammad (S.A.W.) himself is described as
explanation: A Hadith with uninterrupted chain of authorities traced back to the Prophet Muhammad (S.A.W.) himself is described as Mutawātir Hadith.

topics.title: HADITH : AUTHENTICATION OF HADITH

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge (IRK)
q: Which of the following terms is most closely related to divorce?
explanation: 'Iddah' is the term most closely related to divorce in Islamic law. It refers to the waiting period that a woman must observe after the death of her spouse or after a divorce, during which she may not marry another man.

topics.title: FAMILY MATTERS : DIVORCE

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge (IRK)
q: Kaffarah (major expiation) is compulsory upon a husband who revokes
explanation: Kaffarah (major expiation) is compulsory upon a husband who revokes 'zihar'. 'Zihar' is a pre-Islamic Arabian form of divorce where a man says to his wife: 'You are to me as the back of my mother'. This was considered a major sin, and the expiation (kaffarah) for it is described in the Quran.

topics.title: FAMILY MATTERS : DIVORCE

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge (IRK)
q: Part of the wisdom in instituting the 'iddah in marriage is to
explanation: 'Iddah' is a waiting period that a woman must observe after the death of her spouse or after a divorce, during which she may not marry another man. Its primary purpose is to allow for reconciliation between the couple and to determine whether the woman is pregnant, which would impact inheritance and other legal matters.

topics.title: FAMILY MATTERS : DIVORCE

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge (IRK)
q: Zakah rate to be paid on money is
options:
21
4		%

31
2		%

41
2		%

51
2		%
explanation:
The Zakah rate to be paid on money is:

2.5% (or 1/40) of the total amount of money held for one full lunar year (ḥawl), provided it reaches or exceeds the niṣāb (minimum threshold).

topics.title: IBADAT AND THEIR TYPES : ZAKAH

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge (IRK)
q: What percentage of the estate of a late childless wife can the Muslim husband inherit?
explanation: According to Islamic inheritance law, if a wife dies without children, her husband inherits 1/2 of her estate. This is based on the Quranic verse 4:12 which states 'In what your wives leave, your share is a half if they leave no child...'

topics.title: FAMILY MATTERS : INHERITANCE

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge (IRK)
q: The money given out to a woman at the time of contracting her marriage is known as
explanation: The money given out to a woman at the time of contracting her marriage is known as al-Mahar.

topics.title: FAMILY MATTERS : MARRIAGE

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge (IRK)
q: The waiting period for a divorced woman is termed
explanation: The waiting period for a divorced woman in Islam is termed 'Iddah'. This period is to ensure that the woman is not pregnant, but is also a period of mourning and reflection.

topics.title: FAMILY MATTERS : DIVORCE

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge (IRK)
q: When Prophet Muhammad (S.A.W.) led the Muslims in a triumphant entry into Makkah, all Makkans were assured of their safety if they entered the sacred mosque, remained indoors in their homes or
explanation: When Prophet Muhammad (S.A.W.) led the Muslims in a triumphant entry into Makkah, he assured all Makkans of their safety if they entered the sacred mosque, remained indoors in their homes or entered the house of Abu Sufyan. Abu Sufyan was a leader of the Quraysh tribe who later converted to Islam. The Prophet's assurance was a gesture of peace and forgiveness.

topics.title: THE LIFE OF PROPHET MUHAMMAD (SAW) : THE TREATY OF AL-HUDAIBIYYAH AND THE CONQUEST OF MAKKAH

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge (IRK)
q: The Prophet (S.A.W.) ordered some of the Muslims to emigrate to Abyssina
options: in order to be far away from Makkah
because he wanted them to preach Islam to the Negus
in order to go beyonf the reach of their persecutors
because Bilal, one of the converted Muslims was an Alyssinian
explanation: The Prophet (S.A.W.) ordered some of the Muslims to emigrate to Abyssinia in order to go beyond the reach of their persecutors. At that time, Muslims were being persecuted in Makkah, and Abyssinia (modern-day Ethiopia) was known for its just Christian king, Negus, who gave them protection.

topics.title: EARLY CONTACT OF ISLAM WITH AFRICA : HIJRAH TO ABYSSINIA

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge (IRK)
q: The monogamous life of Prophet Muhammad (S.A.W.) lasted for
explanation: The monogamous life of Prophet Muhammad (S.A.W.) lasted for 25 years. During this period, he was married only to Khadijah until her death.

topics.title: THE LIFE OF PROPHET MUHAMMAD (SAW) : HIS BIRTH AND EARLY LIFE

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge (IRK)
q: The Truce of al-Hudaybiyyah included terms such as
options: payment of reparations to the Makkans by the Ummah
prevention of Muslims from entering Makkah until ten years later
the observance by both parties of a ten year period of peace
the encouragement of the Makkans to join the Muslims in al-Madinah
explanation: The Truce of al-Hudaybiyyah included terms such as the observance by both parties of a ten year period of peace. This treaty was between the Prophet Muhammad, representing the state of Medina, and the Quraysh tribe of Mecca.

topics.title: THE LIFE OF PROPHET MUHAMMAD (SAW) : THE TREATY OF AL-HUDAIBIYYAH AND THE CONQUEST OF MAKKAH

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge (IRK)
q: Which of the following led away a section of the Muslims army when preparations were on for the battle of Uhud?
explanation: Abdullah b. Ubayy led away a section of the Muslim army when preparations were on for the battle of Uhud. He was a leader of the hypocrites (munafiqun) who outwardly professed Islam while inwardly concealing disbelief and actively worked against the Muslim community.

topics.title: THE LIFE OF PROPHET MUHAMMAD (SAW) : THE BATTLES OF BADR, UHUD AND KHANDAQ: CAUSES AND EFFECTS

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge (IRK)
q: Which of the following battles resulted in more people being converted to Islam?
explanation: The Battle of Hunayn resulted in more people being converted to Islam. After the conquest of Mecca, the tribes of Hawazin and Thaqif posed a threat to the Muslims. The Prophet Muhammad (PBUH) and the Muslims fought against them in the Battle of Hunayn and emerged victorious. Many captives were taken during this battle, who were later released and converted to Islam.

topics.title: THE LIFE OF PROPHET MUHAMMAD (SAW) : THE BATTLES OF BADR, UHUD AND KHANDAQ: CAUSES AND EFFECTS

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge (IRK)
q: Muhammad resulullah in the kalimatu sh-shahadah means that Muhammad (S.A.W.) is to Allah a
explanation: In the Shahada, 'Muhammad resulullah' means 'Muhammad is the messenger of Allah'. The term 'resul' in Arabic translates to 'messenger'. This is a fundamental belief in Islam that Muhammad (PBUH) is not just a prophet, but the final messenger of Allah.

topics.title: TAWHID AND FIQH : KALIMATUSH-SHAHADAH

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge (IRK)
q: Defrauding by giving too little and asking for too much is condemned in surah
explanation: The practice of defrauding by giving too little and asking for too much is condemned in Surah al-Mutaffifin (also known as at-Tatfif). The Surah takes its name from the word Tatfif in the first verse. This word is used to express the fraudulent act of giving less in measure and weight.

topics.title: ISLAMIC ECONOMIC SYSTEM : AT-TATFIF

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge (IRK)
q: Standing on 'Arafat is a major difference between Hajj and
explanation: The major difference between Hajj and Umrah is the standing on 'Arafat (also known as Wuquf), which is considered the most important part of the Hajj pilgrimage. This ritual is not part of the Umrah. Umrah can be performed at any time of the year, unlike Hajj which has specific dates according to the Islamic calendar.

topics.title: IBADAT AND THEIR TYPES : HAJJ

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge (IRK)
q: When you read loudly while praying Zuhr in one of the rak at, you are expected to
explanation: When you read loudly while praying Zuhr in one of the rak at, you are expected to make a ba di sujud.

topics.title: IBADAT AND THEIR TYPES : SALAH

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge (IRK)
q: Those to benefit from the proceeds of Zakah include
explanation: Zakah is a form of alms-giving treated in Islam as a religious obligation or tax, which, by Quranic ranking, is next after prayer in importance. The proceeds of Zakah are meant to benefit certain categories of people, including those employed to administer it. This is stated in Surah At-Tawbah (9:60).

topics.title: IBADAT AND THEIR TYPES : ZAKAH

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge (IRK)
q: The last sentence in the Talbiyah is
options: Innal Hamda wa n-Ni mata laka wal-mulk
explanation: The last sentence in the Talbiyah is La sharika laka.

topics.title: TAWHID AND FIQH : KALIMATUSH-SHAHADAH

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge (IRK)
q: One of the conditions for performing the Hajj is the
options: ability to read the Quran and write the Quranic verses
explanation: One of the conditions for performing the Hajj is the ability to finance the journey. This is because Hajj is only obligatory for adult Muslims who are physically and financially capable of undertaking the journey, and can support their family during their absence.

topics.title: IBADAT AND THEIR TYPES : HAJJ

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge (IRK)
q: A Muslim who joins the Imam while he is rising from the last ruku of the last rakah has to
options: repeat the raka as if he did not join in it
complete the remaining except the last rakah
continue the prayer and thereafter observe all the rakat
end up the prayer with the Imam
explanation: A Muslim who joins the Imam while he is rising from the last ruku of the last rakah has to continue the prayer and thereafter observe all the rakat.

topics.title: IBADAT AND THEIR TYPES : SALAH

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge (IRK)
q: In which verse of the Quran are woman directed to dress modestly?
explanation: In the Quran, women are directed to dress modestly in Surah An-Nur (24:31). This verse instructs women to guard their private parts and not display their adornment except what is apparent, and to draw their veils over their chests.

topics.title: MORAL LESSONS IN THE GLORIOUS QUR'AN AND HADITH : BEHAVIOUR AND MODESTY IN DRESSING

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge (IRK)
q: Superstitious beliefs are not compatible with Islamic principles because they are
options: usually contrary to the concept of iman
explanation: Superstitious beliefs are not compatible with Islamic principles because they are usually contrary to the concept of iman. Iman in Islam refers to faith or belief in the six articles of faith: belief in God, His angels, His books, His messengers, the Day of Judgment, and destiny. Superstitious beliefs often involve attributing events or outcomes to supernatural forces or luck, which contradicts the Islamic belief in the oneness of God and His control over all events.

topics.title: TAWHID AND FIQH : GENERAL PRACTICES WHICH ARE INCOMPATIBLE WITH ISLAMIC PRINCIPLES OF TAWHID

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge (IRK)
q: Prophet Musa (A.S.) went to the sacred valley where he
explanation: Prophet Musa (A.S.) went to the sacred valley where he received divine directives. This is a reference to the event in the Quran where Prophet Musa (Moses) was called by God at Mount Sinai and given the Ten Commandments.

topics.title: ARTICLE OF FAITH : HIS PROPHETS: ULUL-AZMI

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge (IRK)
q: Kalimatu sh-shahadah is the first pillar of Islam, the second being
explanation: Kalimatu sh-shahadah is the first pillar of Islam, the second being observance of the five daily prayers. The five pillars of Islam are the basic acts of worship that every Muslim is required to perform. They include the shahadah (declaration of faith), salah (prayer), zakah (charity), sawm (fasting during Ramadan), and hajj (pilgrimage to Mecca).

topics.title: TAWHID AND FIQH : KALIMATUSH-SHAHADAH

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge (IRK)
q: What is ijma in Islamic law?
explanation: Ijma in Islamic law refers to the consensus of opinion. It is one of the four main sources of Islamic law, along with the Quran, Hadith, and Qiyas (analogical reasoning). Ijma represents the agreement of Muslim scholars on a particular legal issue.

topics.title: SOURCES AND SCHOOLS OF LAW : THE FOUR MAJOR SOURCES THE QUR'AN, SUNNAH, IJMA AND QIYAS

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge (IRK)
q: The sources of the Shari ah include
explanation: The sources of the Shari'ah include Quran, Ijma (consensus of opinion), and Qiyas (analogical reasoning). These are the primary sources used in the formulation of Islamic law. The Quran is the holy book of Islam, Ijma represents the agreement of Muslim scholars on a particular legal issue, and Qiyas is the process of deductive analogy in which the teachings of the Hadith are compared and contrasted with those of the Quran, in order to apply a known injunction to a new circumstance and create a new injunction.

topics.title: SOURCES AND SCHOOLS OF LAW : THE FOUR MAJOR SOURCES THE QUR'AN, SUNNAH, IJMA AND QIYAS

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge (IRK)
q: Which of the following are secondary sources of the Shari ah?
explanation: The Shari'ah, or Islamic law, is derived from four main sources. The primary sources are the Quran and the Hadith. The secondary sources are Ijma (consensus of scholars) and Qiyas (analogical deduction). Therefore, the correct answer is 'Ijma and Qiyas'.

topics.title: SOURCES AND SCHOOLS OF LAW : THE FOUR MAJOR SOURCES THE QUR'AN, SUNNAH, IJMA AND QIYAS

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge (IRK)
q: Imam Ash-shafii studied under two of his predecessors. These are
explanation: Imam Ash-Shafi'i, one of the four great Imams of Islamic Jurisprudence, studied under Imam Malik and Imam Muhammad ibn al-Hasan al-Shaybani, who was a student of Imam Abu Hanifah. Therefore, the correct answer is 'Malik and Abu Hanifah'.

topics.title: SOURCES AND SCHOOLS OF LAW : THE FOUR SUNNI SCHOOLS OF LAW

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge (IRK)
q: The earliest written records of West African history were written in Arabic because
options: West Africa was colonized by the Arabs
Arabic was the first written language which West Africans came in contact with
several Arabic schools existed in West African ashortly after the advent of Islam
all rulers of West Africa had embraced Islam
explanation: The earliest written records of West African history were written in Arabic because several Arabic schools existed in West African ashortly after the advent of Islam.

topics.title: EARLY CONTACT OF ISLAM WITH AFRICA : THE ROLE OF TRADERS, TEACHERS, PREACHERS, MURABITUN, SUFI ORDERS AND MUJADDIDUN TO THE SPREAD OF ISLAM IN WEST AFRICA.

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge (IRK)
q: The first Islamic university established in West Africa is
explanation: The first Islamic university established in West Africa is Timbuktu.

topics.title: CONTRIBUTIONS OF ISLAM TO EDUCATION : INTELLECTUAL ACTIVITIES OF ISLAM IN WEST AFRICA (DEVELOPMENT OFWRITTEN HISTORY IN ARABIC AND THE ESTABLISHMENT OF SANKORE UNIVERSITY)

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge (IRK)
q: The initial spread of Islam in West Africa can be described as generally
explanation: The initial spread of Islam in West Africa can be described as generally peaceful and systematic.

topics.title: EARLY CONTACT OF ISLAM WITH AFRICA : THE ROLE OF TRADERS, TEACHERS, PREACHERS, MURABITUN, SUFI ORDERS AND MUJADDIDUN TO THE SPREAD OF ISLAM IN WEST AFRICA.

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge (IRK)
q: The jurist who made Tasawwuf compatible with the Shari ah was
explanation: Al-Ghazali is the jurist who made Tasawwuf (Sufism) compatible with the Shari'ah (Islamic law). He was a Persian theologian, jurist, and mystic who is credited with reconciling the mystical aspects of Islam (Sufism) with the legalistic aspects (Shari'ah).

topics.title: CONTRIBUTIONS OF ISLAM TO EDUCATION : THE LIVES AND CONTRIBUTIONS OF IBN SINA, AL-GHAZALI, IBN RUSHDI, AR-RAZIO AND IBN KHALDUN TO EDUCATION.

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge (IRK)
q: The first Umayyad caliph was
explanation: The first Umayyad caliph was Mu'awiya b. Abi Sufyan. The Umayyad dynasty was the first great Muslim dynasty to rule the empire of the Caliphate (661–750 CE), sometimes referred to as the Arab kingdom.

topics.title: THE LIFE OF PROPHET MUHAMMAD (SAW) : ADMINISTRATION OF THE UMMAH AND THE ROLE OF THE MOSQUE (Q.3:159) (Q.4:58 AND 135)

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge (IRK)
q: To ensured the authenticity of the compiled text of the Holy Quran, extracts written on various materials were cross-checked with texts
options: delivered by angel Jibril to the Prophet (S.A.W.)
explanation: To ensure the authenticity of the compiled text of the Holy Quran, extracts written on various materials were cross-checked with texts recalled from memory by early memorizers. This was done to ensure that the written texts matched the oral tradition, which was the primary method of preserving the Quran at the time.

topics.title: PRESERVATION OF THE GLORIOUS QUR’AN : RECORDING, COMPILATION AND STANDARDIZATION OF THE GLORIOUS QUR’AN

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge (IRK)
q: Which of the following terms portrays the Quran as a healing for man's spiritual and moral waywardness?
explanation: The term that portrays the Quran as a healing for man's spiritual and moral waywardness is 'ash-Shifa'. In Arabic, 'ash-Shifa' means 'the healing' and it is used in the Quran to denote its role as a source of spiritual and moral healing.

topics.title: IMPORTANCE OF THE GLORIOUS QUR’AN : AS SOURCE OF GUIDANCE IN SPIRITUAL, MORAL, ECONOMIC, POLITICAL AND SOCIO-CULTURAL MATTERS.

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge (IRK)
q: In Surat al-Maun (Q. 107), we learn that a good believer should not
explanation: In Surat al-Maun (Q. 107), we learn that a good believer should not repulse the orphan. This Surah emphasizes the importance of kindness to orphans and the needy, and criticizes those who are neglectful of their prayers and who show off their religious acts.

topics.title: STUDY OF THE ARABIC TEXT OF THE FOLLOWING SUWAR/AYATS WITH TAJWID : AL-MAUN ((Q.107)

q:
The expression

appears in Surat al-Kafirun
explanation: The expression appears two times in Suratul Kafirun of the Qur'an.

topics.title: STUDY OF THE ARABIC TEXT OF THE FOLLOWING SUWAR/AYATS WITH TAJWID : AL - KAFIRUN

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge (IRK)
q: The expressions Iqra,'allama and al-qalam in Surat al-Alaq emphasize the importance of
explanation: The expressions 'Iqra', 'allama' and 'al-qalam' in Surat al-Alaq emphasize the importance of knowledge. 'Iqra' means 'read', 'allama' means 'taught', and 'al-qalam' means 'the pen'. These expressions are used in the context of learning and education, hence, they emphasize the importance of knowledge.

topics.title: CONTRIBUTIONS OF ISLAM TO EDUCATION : THE GLORIOUS QUR'AN AND HADITH ON EDUCATION

q: Identify the surah to which the following dyah belongs.
explanation: 'ad-Duha' is correct. It is the right opti. Dyah belongs to 'ad-Duha'.

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge (IRK)
q: In the hadith that ends with '. . . piety is based right here . . .', the Prophet (S.A.W.) meanth the
explanation: In the hadith that ends with '. . . piety is based right here . . .', the Prophet (S.A.W.) pointed to his chest, meaning the heart. The heart is often used metaphorically in Islamic tradition to refer to the spiritual heart, the seat of faith and consciousness.

topics.title: HADITH : THE RELATIONSHIP BETWEEN HADITH AND THE GLORIOUS QUR’AN

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge (IRK)
q: Asma ar-rijal is a term used by scholars of
explanation: 'Asma ar-rijal' is a term used by scholars of Hadith. It refers to the biographical evaluation of narrators of Hadith. Scholars of Hadith use this method to determine the reliability and authenticity of Hadiths.

topics.title: HADITH : HISTORY OF HADITH LITERATURE -COLLECTION OF HADITH FROM THE TIME OF THE PROPHET-SAW TO THE PERIOD OF THE SIX AUTHENTIC COLLECTORS OF HADITH

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge (IRK)
q:
'It is most hateful in the sight of Allah that you say that which you do not (Q.61:3)
The main teaching of the verse above is honesty in
explanation: The verse 'It is most hateful in the sight of Allah that you say that which you do not (Q.61:3)' emphasizes honesty in both words and deeds. It condemns hypocrisy and encourages Muslims to be truthful in their words and consistent in their actions.

topics.title: MORAL LESSONS IN THE GLORIOUS QUR'AN AND HADITH : HONESTY

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge (IRK)
q:
The goal of Luqman's counsel to his son:. . .

as contained in surah 31: 13 is to
explanation: Luqman's counsel to his son warn against finding partners for Allah.

topics.title: MORAL LESSONS IN THE GLORIOUS QUR'AN AND HADITH : GENERAL MORAL LESSONS CONTAINED IN THE ADMONITION OF SAGE LUQMAN TO HIS SON

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge (IRK)
q: Imama Malik Ibn Anas was born in
explanation: Imam Malik Ibn Anas, the founder of the Maliki school of thought, was born in al-Madinah. He lived and taught in al-Madinah for most of his life.

topics.title: HADITH : AUTHENTICATION OF HADITH

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge (IRK)
q: The authenticity of a hadith may be faulted if
options: its wording is too close to the text of the Quran
it is longer than a verse of the Quran
explanation: The authenticity of a Hadith can be faulted if it contradicts a Quranic injunction. The Hadith are sayings and actions of Prophet Muhammad and they should not contradict the Quran, which is the word of God in Islam. If a Hadith contradicts the Quran, it is likely not authentic.

topics.title: HADITH : AUTHENTICATION OF HADITH

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge (IRK)
q: In the Islamic belief system, the opposite of Tawhid is
explanation: In the Islamic belief system, the opposite of Tawhid (the oneness of God) is Shirk. Shirk refers to the sin of practicing idolatry or polytheism, i.e., the deification or worship of anyone or anything other than the singular God.

topics.title: TAWHID AND FIQH : SHIRK

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge (IRK)
q: The divine attribute of omnipotence is implied in
explanation: The divine attribute of omnipotence is implied in al-Qadir. In Islamic theology, al-Qadir is one of the 99 names of Allah and it means 'The All-Powerful' or 'The Most Able', which implies omnipotence.

topics.title: ARTICLE OF FAITH : BELIEF IN ALLAH

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge (IRK)
q:
'Say: I am but a man like yourselves, (but) The inspiration has come to me ...' (Q.18:110).
In relation to Kalimat sh-shahadah, the verse quoted above
options: proves that any other prophet could take the place of Muhammad (S.A.W.)
explanation: The verse 'Say: I am but a man like yourselves, (but) The inspiration has come to me ...' (Q.18:110) emphasizes a part of the content of Kalimat sh-shahadah. It emphasizes the humanity of Prophet Muhammad and the divine inspiration he received, which is a part of the content of the Shahada, the Islamic creed.

topics.title: TAWHID AND FIQH : KALIMATUSH-SHAHADAH

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge (IRK)
q:
'Still less can their knowledge comprehend the Hereafter . . .' (Q.27:66).
This verse of the Quran implies that
options: man's knowledge of the Hereafter can only be partial
most men do not believe in the Hereafter
man's comprehension of the expectations in the Hereafter is always on the decrease
it is difficult to believe in the Hereafter
explanation: This verse of the Quran implies that most men do not believe in the Hereafter.

topics.title: ARTICLE OF FAITH : BELIEF IN ALLAH

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge (IRK)
q: According to Islami teachings, angels are
explanation: According to Islamic teachings, angels are considered as loyal servants of Allah. They are created from light and are incapable of disobedience. They carry out the commands of Allah without question.

topics.title: ARTICLE OF FAITH : BELIEF IN ALLAH'S ANGELS

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge (IRK)
q: The prophet whose wife was impious and consequently suffered the same calamity with the unbelievers was
explanation: The prophet whose wife was impious and consequently suffered the same calamity with the unbelievers was Prophet Lut. His wife did not believe in his message and was consequently punished along with the unbelievers.

topics.title: ARTICLE OF FAITH : HIS PROPHETS: ULUL-AZMI

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge (IRK)
q: The prophet whose son was drowned in a flood along with the unbelievers was
explanation: The prophet whose son was drowned in a flood along with the unbelievers was Prophet Nuh (Noah). His son did not believe in his message and was consequently drowned in the flood that was sent as a punishment for the unbelievers.

topics.title: ARTICLE OF FAITH : THE LAST DAY: YAWM-AL-BA'TH

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge (IRK)
q: An aspect of the Nigerian culture which may be regarded as shirk is
options: ceremonies involving social interaction between boys and girls
explanation: Shirk in Islam is the sin of practicing idolatry or polytheism, i.e., the deification or worship of anyone or anything other than the singular God, Allah. Therefore, the worship of ancestors, which is an aspect of Nigerian culture, can be regarded as shirk.

topics.title: TAWHID AND FIQH : SHIRK

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge (IRK)
q: According to Quran 4: 48, to set up partners with Allah is a great offence which Allah will not forgive
options: until He has forgiven all other sins
even if He forgives all other sins
explanation: According to Quran 4: 48, to set up partners with Allah is a great offence which Allah will not forgive except the offender repents .

topics.title: ARTICLE OF FAITH : BELIEF IN ALLAH

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge (IRK)
q: Fortune telling is un-Islamic
options: only when it is done by non-Muslims
when Islamic scriptures are not used for it
only in matters relating to religious practices
at all times and in all matters
explanation: Fortune telling is considered un-Islamic at all times and in all matters. This is because it is believed to involve the prediction of the future, which is known only to Allah. Therefore, it is seen as an attempt to know what is in the realm of the unseen, which is considered a form of disbelief.

topics.title: TAWHID AND FIQH : GENERAL PRACTICES WHICH ARE INCOMPATIBLE WITH ISLAMIC PRINCIPLES OF TAWHID

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge (IRK)
q: In Surat al-falaq, the Quran refers to an element of witchcraft and the
explanation: In Surat al-falaq, the Quran refers to the mischief of the envious one as an element of witchcraft. The Surah is a prayer for protection from various evils, including the evil of the envious when he envies.

topics.title: ARTICLE OF FAITH : BELIEF IN ALLAH'S ANGELS

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge (IRK)
q: Accountability is an act of worship
options: though it is not mentioned in the Quran
because it is specifically mentioned in the Quran
being one of the fundamental principles of Islam
explanation: Accountability is an act of worship because it is specifically mentioned in the Quran.

topics.title: IBADAT AND THEIR TYPES : GOOD DEEDS 26TH HADITH OF AN-NAWAWI

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge (IRK)
q: The second hadith in an-Nawawi's collection teaches that it is righteousness to worship Allah as if
options: He would punish one on the spot
explanation: The second hadith in an-Nawawi's collection teaches that it is righteousness to worship Allah as if one was seeing Him physically. This is a metaphorical expression that encourages Muslims to worship Allah with utmost sincerity and devotion, as if they are seeing Him, even though they cannot see Him.

topics.title: HADITH : THE STUDY OF THE ARABIC TEXTS OF THE FOLLOWING AHADITH FROMAN-NAWAWI’S COLLECTION: 1,3,5,6,7,9,10,11,12,13,15,16, 18,19,21,22,25,27,34, AND 41

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge (IRK)
q: At the end of his prayer, how many sittings for at-tashahud are expected of someone who misses two rakats in a congregational solat al-Maghrib?
explanation: If someone misses two rakats in a congregational solat al-Maghrib, they are expected to have three sittings for at-tashahud at the end of their prayer. The first sitting is after the completion of the first two rakats, the second sitting is after the third rakat, and the third sitting is after making up the missed rakats.

topics.title: IBADAT AND THEIR TYPES : SALAH

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge (IRK)
q: An adult Muslim travels 300km by road during the month of Ramadan and arrives at his destination on the same day. He spends three days and returns on the fifth day. He is allowed to defer his fasting of the
explanation: He is allowed to defer his fasting of the last day.

topics.title: IBADAT AND THEIR TYPES : SAWM

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge (IRK)
q: The text of talbiyah in hajj emphasizes the pilgrim's
options: readiness to observe the rites of hajj to the end
realization of the uniqueness of the rites of hajj
explanation: The text of Talbiyah in Hajj is 'Labbayka Allahumma Labbayk. Labbayk La Shareeka Laka Labbayk. Inna l-Hamda, Wa n-Ni'mata, Laka wal Mulk, La Shareeka Lak.' (Here I am, O Allah, here I am. Here I am, You have no partner, here I am. Indeed, all praise, grace and sovereignty belong to You. You have no partner.) This emphasizes the pilgrim's recognition of Allah's absolute authority.

topics.title: IBADAT AND THEIR TYPES : HAJJ

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge (IRK)
q: A woman in her menstrual period
options: is not exempted from all forms of ibadah
is exempted from all forms of ibadah
explanation: A woman in her menstrual period is not exempted from all forms of ibadah (acts of worship), though she is exempted from certain specific ones.

topics.title: IBADAT AND THEIR TYPES : TAHARAH, ITS TYPES AND IMPORTANCE AL-ISTINJA'/ISTIJMAR, ALWUDU', AT-TAYAMMUM AND AL-GHUSL HADITH 10 AND 23 OF AN-NAWAWI

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge (IRK)
q: Quran 2: 193 enjoins believers to fight unbelievers in the course of Allah if it takes that to
explanation: Quran 2:193 says 'And fight them until there is no fitnah and [until] the religion, all of it, is for Allah. And if they cease - then indeed, Allah is Seeing of what they do.' This verse enjoins believers to fight unbelievers in the course of Allah if it takes that to defend themselves against transgression (fitnah).

topics.title: ARTICLE OF FAITH : BELIEF IN ALLAH

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge (IRK)
q: According to Quran 6: 160, while bad deeds would be rewarded in the same measure, good deeds would be rewarded
explanation: According to Quran 6:160, 'Whoever comes [on the Day of Judgement] with a good deed will have ten times the like thereof [to his credit], and whoever comes with an evil deed will not be recompensed except the like thereof; and they will not be wronged.' This verse clearly states that while bad deeds would be rewarded in the same measure, good deeds would be rewarded ten-fold.

topics.title: IBADAT AND THEIR TYPES : GOOD DEEDS 26TH HADITH OF AN-NAWAWI

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge (IRK)
q: The doctrine of trinity is regarded in Islam as a form of
explanation: In Islam, the doctrine of trinity is regarded as a form of 'shirk'. Shirk in Islam is the sin of practicing idolatry or polytheism, i.e., the deification or worship of anyone or anything besides the singular God, Allah. In this case, the doctrine of trinity which posits the existence of three persons in one God is considered as associating partners with Allah.

topics.title: TAWHID AND FIQH : SHIRK

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge (IRK)
q: While polygamy is permitted in Surah 4:3, Surah 4: 129
explanation: Surah 4:129 in the Quran highlights the inherent problems in polygamy. It states that 'You will never be able to do perfect justice between wives even if it is your ardent desire'. This verse is often interpreted as a caution against polygamy, highlighting the difficulties in treating multiple wives equally.

topics.title: FAMILY MATTERS : DIVORCE

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge (IRK)
q: The practice of comparing the back of one's wife to that of one's mother as a method of divorce was known as
explanation: The practice of comparing the back of one's wife to that of one's mother as a method of divorce was known as 'zihar'. Zihar was a pre-Islamic form of divorce where a man would say to his wife, 'You are to me like the back of my mother'. This was considered a form of divorce in pre-Islamic Arabia but was abolished in Islam.

topics.title: FAMILY MATTERS : DIVORCE

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge (IRK)
q: In Islamic law on divorce, the custody of a child aged two years or less is normally conceded to the
explanation: In Islamic law on divorce, the custody of a child aged two years or less is normally conceded to the mother. This is because at such a young age, the child is believed to need the mother's care more. However, this can vary depending on the circumstances and the best interests of the child.

topics.title: FAMILY MATTERS : DIVORCE

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge (IRK)
q: In Islamic law of inheritance, the brother of a deceased person who had a son is to
options: take a larger share of the inheritance
take half of the share of the son
explanation: In Islamic law of inheritance, the brother of a deceased person who had a son is to be excluded from inheritance. This is because the son is considered a direct heir, and the presence of a direct heir excludes the siblings from inheritance. The brother can only inherit if there are no direct heirs (children or grandchildren) or if the deceased person left a will specifying a share for the brother.

topics.title: FAMILY MATTERS : INHERITANCE

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge (IRK)
q: Which one of the following actions may be regarded as Mubah?
explanation: Shaking hands while greeting may be regarded as Mubah.

topics.title: TAWHID AND FIQH : GENERAL PRACTICES WHICH ARE INCOMPATIBLE WITH ISLAMIC PRINCIPLES OF TAWHID

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge (IRK)
q: The shariah contrasts with the common law because the former is
explanation: Shariah law is considered universal because it is believed to be a divine law applicable to all Muslims, regardless of their geographical location or culture. It is not subject to change over time or place, unlike common law which can vary from place to place.

topics.title: SOURCES AND SCHOOLS OF LAW : THE FOUR SUNNI SCHOOLS OF LAW

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge (IRK)
q: The Sunni school of thought noted for resorting frequently to the use of personal opinion was that of Imam
explanation: The Sunni school of thought noted for resorting frequently to the use of personal opinion was that of Imam Shafii.

topics.title: SOURCES AND SCHOOLS OF LAW : THE FOUR SUNNI SCHOOLS OF LAW

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge (IRK)
q: In the Islamic economic system, baytul mal is equivalent to the
explanation: In the Islamic economic system, Baytul Mal refers to the public treasury. It is a financial institution responsible for the administration of taxes in an Islamic state, among other duties. It serves as a treasury for the Muslim community, where the revenues are collected and distributed.

topics.title: ISLAMIC ECONOMIC SYSTEM : BAITUL-MAL AS AN INSTITUTION OF SOCIO-ECONOMIC WELFARE

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge (IRK)
q: '. . . And consult them in the affairs . . .' (Quran 3: 159). This expression emphasizes the importance of the principle of
explanation: The verse from the Quran (3:159) emphasizes the principle of Shura, which means consultation. It is a principle in Islam that decisions should be made by consultation among those concerned. It is a method of decision-making, including in political and administrative affairs.

topics.title: THE LIFE OF PROPHET MUHAMMAD (SAW) : ADMINISTRATION OF THE UMMAH AND THE ROLE OF THE MOSQUE (Q.3:159) (Q.4:58 AND 135)

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge (IRK)
q: The pre-Islamic practice of shigar which was abolished by Islam meant
explanation: Shigar was a pre-Islamic practice where a man would give his sister in marriage to another man, and in return, he would marry the other man's sister without a dowry. Islam abolished this practice as it was considered unfair and unjust.

topics.title: TAWHID AND FIQH : GENERAL PRACTICES WHICH ARE INCOMPATIBLE WITH ISLAMIC PRINCIPLES OF TAWHID

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge (IRK)
q: In the course of hijjat'l-wida, Muslims were taught to
options: ensure promptness and impartiality in the dispensation of the justice
explanation: During the Farewell Pilgrimage (Hijjat'l-wida), Prophet Muhammad emphasized on the rights of women and the kind treatment of domestic servants. He also stressed on the importance of fulfilling the rights of others and maintaining good character.

topics.title: THE LIFE OF PROPHET MUHAMMAD (SAW) : HIJJATUL-WADA THE FAREWELL PILGRIMAGE SERMON, AND LESSONS.

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge (IRK)
q: Compared with the situation in Makkah before Hijrah, the dawah in al-Madina was more
explanation: The dawah (invitation to Islam) in al-Madina was more far-reaching compared to Makkah. In Makkah, the Muslims were a persecuted minority, but in Madina, they formed a community with political authority. This allowed the message of Islam to spread more widely.

topics.title: THE LIFE OF PROPHET MUHAMMAD (SAW) : DA’WAH IN MAKKAH AND MADINAH

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge (IRK)
q: The Caliph who appointed the Shura Council to produce a successor after his death was
explanation: Caliph Umar b. Khattab appointed a Shura Council to choose his successor after his death. The Shura (consultation) Council was a group of notable companions of the Prophet who were tasked with the responsibility of choosing the next Caliph.

topics.title: THE RIGHTLY GUIDED CALIPHS AL-KHULAFA'U RASHIDUN : THE LIVES AND CONTRIBUTIONS OF THE FOUR RIGHTLY GUIDED CALIPHS

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge (IRK)
q: The earliest contact of Islam with Africa dates back to hte period of
explanation: The earliest contact of Islam with Africa dates back to the period of Prophet Muhammad b. Abdullah. During his time, some Muslims migrated to Abyssinia (modern day Ethiopia) to escape persecution in Makkah. This was the first Hijrah (migration) in Islam.

topics.title: EARLY CONTACT OF ISLAM WITH AFRICA : HIJRAH TO ABYSSINIA

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge (IRK)
q: Ihya 'Uhlum ad-din is the title of a book written by
explanation: Ihya 'Uhlum ad-din is a book written by Al-Ghazali. Al-Ghazali was a Persian theologian, jurist, and philosopher. His book, Ihya 'Uhlum ad-din, is considered one of the most read books in the Islamic world, covering various aspects of Islam including theology, ethics, and spirituality.

topics.title: CONTRIBUTIONS OF ISLAM TO EDUCATION : THE LIVES AND CONTRIBUTIONS OF IBN SINA, AL-GHAZALI, IBN RUSHDI, AR-RAZIO AND IBN KHALDUN TO EDUCATION.

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge (IRK)
q: One of the ways by which Islamic civilization was manifested in West Africa was
options: the employment of scholars of Arabic as envoys by kings and emperors
construction of palaces based on Arab technology
recruitment of Muslim medical personnel to man health institutions
explanation: One of the ways Islamic civilization was manifested in West Africa was through the employment of scholars of Arabic as envoys by kings and emperors. This was a common practice during the time of the Islamic empires, as Arabic scholars were highly respected and their knowledge was sought after. This helped to spread Islamic culture and influence throughout West Africa.

topics.title: THE IMPACT OF ISLAM IN WEST AFRICA : THE INFLUENCE OF ISLAM ON THE SOCIO-POLITICAL LIFE OF SOME WEST AFRICAN EMPIRES: GHANA, MALI, SONGHAI AND BORNO

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge (IRK)
q: Ahmad Baba, one of the early West African Ulama, was based in
explanation: Ahmad Baba, one of the early West African Ulama, was based in Timbuktu. Timbuktu was a major center of Islamic learning and culture during the Middle Ages, and Ahmad Baba was one of its most prominent scholars.

topics.title: CONTRIBUTIONS OF ISLAM TO EDUCATION : INTELLECTUAL ACTIVITIES OF AHMAD BABA OF TIMBUKTU, SHEIKHAL-MAGHILI, SHEIKH USMAN DANFODIO, SULTAN MUHAMMAD BELLO AND IBN BATTUTA

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge (IRK)
q: Ignorance of religious rites and duties is not excusable in Islam because
options: it is obligatory on all Muslims to seek knowledge
most Muslims can read and write Arabic
books of Islamic jurisprudence have been translated into many languages
the Islamic system of education is highly developed all over the world
explanation: Ignorance of religious rites and duties is not excusable in Islam because it is obligatory on all Muslims to seek knowledge. This is based on the teachings of the Prophet Muhammad, who said, 'Seeking knowledge is obligatory upon every Muslim.' This includes knowledge of religious rites and duties.

topics.title: CONTRIBUTIONS OF ISLAM TO EDUCATION : THE AIMS AND OBJECTIVES OF ISLAMIC EDUCATION

q: Al-bayyinah appears in the Holy Quran chapter 98
explanation: Al-bayyinah appears in the Holy Quran chapter 98 twice.

q:
The verse

(Q. 99: 4)

explanation: The verse (Q. 99: 4) translates as 'On that Day will she declare her tidings.

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge (IRK)
q: The major subject of the first revelation of Prophet Muhammad (S.A.W.) is
explanation: The first revelation of Prophet Muhammad (S.A.W.) is Surah Al-Alaq (96:1-5) which emphasizes on 'Read' and 'knowledge'. Therefore, the major subject of the first revelation is 'education'.

topics.title: THE LIFE OF PROPHET MUHAMMAD (SAW) : HIS CALL TO PROPHETHOOD

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge (IRK)
q: Which of the following sets, according to Ayatul Kursiyy, is 'Allah not susceptible to ?
explanation: Ayatul Kursiyy (Quran 2:255) mentions that 'Allah! There is no deity except Him, the Ever-Living, the Sustainer of existence. Neither drowsiness overtakes Him nor sleep.' Therefore, Allah is not susceptible to 'Slumber or sleep'.

topics.title: STUDY OF THE ARABIC TEXT OF THE FOLLOWING SUWAR/AYATS WITH TAJWID : AYATUL-KURSIY

q: This Hadith teaches us to aviod
explanation: "This Hadith warns believers to stay away from innovation."
