subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge (IRK)
q:
'Of the excellence of the Islam of a man is his leaving alon what does not concern him'.
The above statement is a
explanation: The statement 'Of the excellence of the Islam of a man is his leaving alone what does not concern him' is a Hadith (prophetic tradition) narrated by Tirmidhi. Therefore, it is a 'prophetic tradition'.

topics.title: HADITH : THE STUDY OF THE ARABIC TEXTS OF THE FOLLOWING AHADITH FROMAN-NAWAWI’S COLLECTION: 1,3,5,6,7,9,10,11,12,13,15,16, 18,19,21,22,25,27,34, AND 41

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge (IRK)
q: 'Aishah's role in the transmission of Hadith is next in significance to that of
explanation: 'Aishah's role in the transmission of Hadith is significant. However, the companion who narrated the most Hadiths is Abu Hurayrah. Therefore, 'Aishah's role in the transmission of Hadith is next in significance to that of 'Abu Hurayrah'.

topics.title: HADITH : HISTORY OF HADITH LITERATURE -COLLECTION OF HADITH FROM THE TIME OF THE PROPHET-SAW TO THE PERIOD OF THE SIX AUTHENTIC COLLECTORS OF HADITH

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge (IRK)
q: The surah which declares that there is no compromise in religion is
explanation: Surah al-Kafirun (109:1-6) declares that there is no compromise in religion. It ends with 'For you is your religion, and for me is my religion.' Therefore, the surah which declares that there is no compromise in religion is 'al-Kafirun'.

topics.title: STUDY OF THE ARABIC TEXT OF THE FOLLOWING SUWAR/AYATS WITH TAJWID : AL - KAFIRUN

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge (IRK)
q: Islam regards the whole body of a woman as 'awrah except her face and
explanation: In Islam, the term 'awrah refers to the parts of the body that are not supposed to be exposed in public. For women, this generally includes the entire body except for the face and hands. Therefore, the correct answer is 'hands'.

topics.title: FAMILY MATTERS : MARRIAGE

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge (IRK)
q:
'Verily spendthrifts are brothers
Of the Evil ones; ... .' (Q. 17:27).
In the verse above, 'Allah compares a wasteful person to the evil ones because the former
explanation: In the verse from the Quran (17:27), Allah compares spendthrifts to the evil ones because they are ungrateful to the provider. Spendthrifts waste the resources provided to them without appreciating their value, which is seen as a form of ingratitude in Islam.

topics.title: MORAL LESSONS IN THE GLORIOUS QUR’AN AND HADITH : EXTRAVAGANCE (Q.17:26-27) (Q.31:18-19)

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge (IRK)
q: Which of the following was the most preferred method of preserving the Holy Quran during the time of the rightly guided caliphs?
explanation: Writing and compilation into a standardized Mushaf was the most preferred and effective method of preservation during the time of the Rightly Guided Caliphs.

topics.title: PRESERVATION OF THE GLORIOUS QUR’AN : THE ROLE PLAYED BY THECOMPANIONS OF THE PROPHET (SAW)

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge (IRK)
q: The collections regarded as the most authentic in Hadith literature are those of
explanation: The collections of Hadiths (sayings and actions of Prophet Muhammad) that are considered the most authentic in Islamic literature are those of Bukhari and Muslim. These collections are highly respected and widely used in the Islamic world.

topics.title: HADITH : THE SIX SOUND COLLECTORS OF HADITH – BIOGRAPHIES AND THEIR WORKS.

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge (IRK)
q: The Muwatta of Imam Malik can be described as a book of
explanation: The Muwatta of Imam Malik is a collection of Hadith and Fiqh (Islamic jurisprudence). It is one of the earliest written collections of Hadith and Fiqh and is considered one of the most authentic sources of these disciplines.

topics.title: HADITH : MUWATTA AND ITS AUTHOR – THE BIOGRAPHY OF IMAM MALIK AND THE STUDY OF HIS BOOK

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge (IRK)
q:
The verse

(Q. 5:1)
requires believers to
explanation: The verse requires believers tofulfil all obligations.

topics.title: MORAL LESSONS IN THE GLORIOUS QUR’AN AND HADITH : TRUST AND OBLIGATIONS (Q:4:58) (Q.5:1) AND PROMISES (Q.16:91) HADITH ‘HE HAS (REALLY) NO FAITH ….. NOT FULFILLED HIS PROMISE” (BAIHAQI)

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge (IRK)
q: 'If anyone sees an abomination ... .' The Hadith above saddles man with the responsibility of
explanation: The Hadith 'If anyone sees an abomination ... .' implies that if a person sees something wrong or evil, they should try to stop it or resist it. Therefore, the responsibility it saddles man with is resisting evil practices.

topics.title: MORAL LESSONS IN THE GLORIOUS QUR'AN AND HADITH : ENJOINING WHAT IS GOOD AND FORBIDDING WHAT IS WRONG HADITH 25 AND 34 OF AN NAWAWI

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge (IRK)
q: The attribute of 'Allah which emphasizes His holiness is
explanation: In Islam, 'Al-Qudus' is one of the 99 names of Allah, which means 'The Holy'. Therefore, the attribute of Allah which emphasizes His holiness is 'Al-Qudus'.

topics.title: ARTICLE OF FAITH : BELIEF IN ALLAH

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge (IRK)
q: In view of the various favours bestowed on man by 'Allah as enumerated in surah 56: 57-74, man is enjoined to shows gratitude to Him by
explanation: In view of the various favours bestowed on man by 'Allah as enumerated in surah 56: 57-74, man is enjoined to shows gratitude to Him by celebrating His name with praises.

topics.title: ARTICLE OF FAITH : BELIEF IN ALLAH

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge (IRK)
q: According to the Holy Quran (27:59-66) nature is a unique manifestation of the work of Allah because
options: man has no problem coping with it
man has continuously endeavoured to conquer it
it is beyond the scope of human comprehension
the Quran gives details of several aspects of it
explanation: According to the Holy Quran (27:59-66) nature is a unique manifestation of the work of Allah because the Quran gives details of several aspects of it.

topics.title: ARTICLE OF FAITH : BELIEF IN ALLAH

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge (IRK)
q: The angle that interrogates the dead in the grave is
explanation: In Islamic belief, the angel that interrogates the dead in the grave is Nakir. Along with Munkar, Nakir is responsible for testing individuals in the grave on their faith and life's deeds.

topics.title: ARTICLE OF FAITH : BELIEF IN ALLAH'S ANGELS

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge (IRK)
q: Ibrahim is described in the Holy Quran as
explanation: Ibrahim is described in the Holy Quran as 'Hanif'. The term 'Hanif' refers to someone who is a monotheist, someone who believes in one God. This is in line with the Islamic belief that Prophet Ibrahim (Abraham) was a monotheist and he preached monotheism.

topics.title: ARTICLE OF FAITH : BELIEF IN ALLAH

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge (IRK)
q: Quran 22:31 likens one who assigns partners to Allah to that who had fallen from heaven and was snatched up by
explanation: Quran 22:31 likens one who assigns partners to Allah to that who had fallen from heaven and was snatched up by birds.

topics.title: ARTICLE OF FAITH : BELIEF IN ALLAH

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge (IRK)
q: According to Hadith 36 in an-Nawawi's collection. 'Allah is ready to aid any servant so long as the servant is ready to aid his
explanation: According to Hadith 36 in an-Nawawi's collection, 'Allah is ready to aid any servant so long as the servant is ready to aid his brothers'. This Hadith emphasizes the importance of helping and supporting one's fellow Muslims.

topics.title: HADITH : THE STUDY OF THE ARABIC TEXTS OF THE FOLLOWING AHADITH FROMAN-NAWAWI’S COLLECTION: 1,3,5,6,7,9,10,11,12,13,15,16, 18,19,21,22,25,27,34, AND 41

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge (IRK)
q: With the institution of zakah, the distribution of wealth within the society should become
explanation: With the institution of zakah, the distribution of wealth within the society should become legally binding.

topics.title: IBADAT AND THEIR TYPES : ZAKAH

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge (IRK)
q: Under tje < aliki school of law, the minimum number of people required for Jumu'ah prayer is
explanation: Under tje < aliki school of law, the minimum number of people required for Jumu'ah prayer is twelve.

topics.title: IBADAT AND THEIR TYPES : SALAH

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge (IRK)
q: One of the spiritual objectives of as-salah is that it
explanation: As-salah, or the Islamic prayer, is a spiritual practice that helps Muslims develop self-restraint. It is a form of worship that requires discipline, concentration, and humility, all of which contribute to self-restraint. The other options, while potentially beneficial outcomes of regular prayer, are not its primary spiritual objectives.

topics.title: IBADAT AND THEIR TYPES : SALAH

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge (IRK)
q: In which year of the Hijrah was fasting introduced in Islam?
explanation: Fasting in Islam was introduced in the 2nd year of the Hijrah (2 AH).

topics.title: IBADAT AND THEIR TYPES : SAWM

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge (IRK)
q: One major aim of marriage in Islam is to
options: increase the working force of the family
distinguish between the duties of male and female
explanation: One of the major aims of marriage in Islam is to achieve legitimate procreation. Marriage is seen as a means to continue the human race and create a family unit where children are brought up in a morally sound environment. The other options do not accurately represent the primary aims of marriage in Islam.

topics.title: FAMILY MATTERS : MARRIAGE

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge (IRK)
q: The term Khitbah in marriage contract means
explanation: The term Khitbah in marriage contract means formal approach.

topics.title: FAMILY MATTERS : MARRIAGE

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge (IRK)
q: Zihar means
options: likening a wife's back to that of one's mother
forcing a woman to marry against her wish
explanation: Zihar is an Arabic term that refers to a pre-Islamic Arabian form of divorce, in which a man likened his wife's back to that of his mother's. This practice was prohibited in Islam. The other options do not accurately define the term 'Zihar'.

topics.title: FAMILY MATTERS : DIVORCE

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge (IRK)
q: The most hateful of the permissible things in the sight of 'Allah is
explanation: In Islam, the most hateful of the permissible things in the sight of Allah is divorce (talaq). This is based on a Hadith of the Prophet Muhammad (PBUH) where he said, 'The most detestable of lawful things before Allah is divorce.' (Sunan Ibn Majah)

topics.title: FAMILY MATTERS : DIVORCE

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge (IRK)
q: In Islamic law of inheritance, the share of the female child is
explanation: In Islamic law of inheritance, the share of the female child is half of the male's. This is based on the Quranic verse (4:11) which states that 'Allah commands you as regards your children's (inheritance); to the male, a portion equal to that of two females.'

topics.title: FAMILY MATTERS : INHERITANCE

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge (IRK)
q: If a deceased Muslim had made a will on his property, which of the following is disqualified from benefiting from it?
explanation: In Islamic law, if a deceased Muslim had made a will on his property, his son is disqualified from benefiting from it. This is because the son is already an automatic heir and will receive a share of the inheritance. The will (wasiyyah) is meant for those who are not automatic heirs.

topics.title: FAMILY MATTERS : INHERITANCE

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge (IRK)
q: In the law of inheritance, the term al-wasiyyah refers to
explanation: In the law of inheritance, the term al-wasiyyah refers to a bequest. This is a provision in a person's will where they specify who should receive a portion of their property after their death. It is a way to distribute wealth to those who are not automatic heirs.

topics.title: FAMILY MATTERS : INHERITANCE

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge (IRK)
q: In Sharrah makruh is an action which is
explanation: In Shariah, makruh is an action which is discouraged but not punished. It refers to actions that are not sinful but are better to be avoided. If a person performs a makruh action, they will not be punished, but if they avoid it, they will be rewarded.

topics.title: TAWHID AND FIQH : GENERAL PRACTICES WHICH ARE INCOMPATIBLE WITH ISLAMIC PRINCIPLES OF TAWHID

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge (IRK)
q: In pre-Islamic period, the social practice of tribalism was known as
explanation: In pre-Islamic period, the social practice of tribalism was known as al-asabiyyah. Al-asabiyyah refers to social solidarity with an emphasis on unity, group consciousness and sense of shared purpose, and social cohesion, originally in a context of 'tribalism' and 'clanism'.

topics.title: ISLAMIC HISTORY AND CIVILIZATION - PRE-ISLAMIC ARABIA JAHILIYYAH : JAHILIYYAH PRACTICES: IDOL WORSHIP, INFANTICIDE, POLYANDRY, GAMBLING, USURY, ETC.

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge (IRK)
q: In the Islamic political system, Mas'uliyyah means
explanation: In the Islamic political system, Mas'uliyyah means accountability. It is a concept that refers to the duty of a person to take responsibility for their actions and to be accountable for them.

topics.title: THE LIFE OF PROPHET MUHAMMAD (SAW) : ADMINISTRATION OF THE UMMAH AND THE ROLE OF THE MOSQUE (Q.3:159) (Q.4:58 AND 135)

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge (IRK)
q: The outcome of the Battle of Badr proved that
explanation: The outcome of the Battle of Badr proved that Allah supported the Muslim army. Despite being outnumbered, the Muslim army emerged victorious, which was seen as a sign of divine support.

topics.title: THE LIFE OF PROPHET MUHAMMAD (SAW) : THE BATTLES OF BADR, UHUD AND KHANDAQ: CAUSES AND EFFECTS

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge (IRK)
q: The second treaty of al-Aqabah was a turning point because Prophet Muhammad (S.A.W.)
explanation: The second treaty of al-Aqabah was a turning point because Prophet Muhammad (S.A.W.) agreed to emigrate to Yathrib. This event, known as the Hijrah, marked the beginning of the Islamic calendar and the establishment of the first Islamic state in Medina.

topics.title: THE LIFE OF PROPHET MUHAMMAD (SAW) : THE HIJRAH

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge (IRK)
q: The Prophet (S.A.W) died in the year
explanation: The Prophet (S.A.W) died in the year 632 C.E. This marked the end of the prophetic era and the beginning of the caliphate.

topics.title: THE LIFE OF PROPHET MUHAMMAD (SAW) : HIS BIRTH AND EARLY LIFE

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge (IRK)
q: The Muslim ruler of Mali who embarked on a celebrated pilgrimage to Makkah in the 11th Century C.E. was
explanation: Mansa Musa was the Muslim ruler of Mali who embarked on a celebrated pilgrimage to Makkah in the 11th Century C.E. He was known for his enormous wealth and his pilgrimage to Mecca, during which he spent lavishly, causing significant inflation in the regions through which he passed.

topics.title: THE IMPACT OF ISLAM IN WEST AFRICA : THE INFLUENCE OF ISLAM ON THE SOCIO-POLITICAL LIFE OF SOME WEST AFRICAN EMPIRES: GHANA, MALI, SONGHAI AND BORNO

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge (IRK)
q: By the first pact of al-Aqabah, the delegates promised Prophet Muhammad (S.A.W.) to become
explanation: By the first pact of al-Aqabah, the delegates promised Prophet Muhammad (S.A.W.) to become believers in Allah.

topics.title: THE LIFE OF PROPHET MUHAMMAD (SAW) : HIS CALL TO PROPHETHOOD

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge (IRK)
q: The Battle of Khandaq was known as such because the
options: Muslims dug a trench as a war strategy
explanation: The Battle of Khandaq, also known as the Battle of the Trench, was named so because the Muslims dug a trench as a defensive strategy against the Quraysh and their allies. The trench prevented the enemy cavalry from launching an effective assault, hence the name.

topics.title: THE LIFE OF PROPHET MUHAMMAD (SAW) : THE BATTLES OF BADR, UHUD AND KHANDAQ: CAUSES AND EFFECTS

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge (IRK)
q: The first male adult to embrace Islam after the commencement of Islamic da'wah in Makkah was
explanation: Abu Bakr was the first adult male to embrace Islam after the commencement of Islamic da'wah in Makkah. He was a close friend of Prophet Muhammad and later became the first Caliph of the Islamic community after Muhammad's death.

topics.title: THE LIFE OF PROPHET MUHAMMAD (SAW) : HIS CALL TO PROPHETHOOD

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge (IRK)
q: In waht ways did the Muslim leaders contribute to the spread of Islam in West Africa?
options: Their moral standard and mode of worship impressed the local people
They sold commodities which were essential and indispensable
Acceptance of Islam was made a major condition for the exchange of goods
Organization of regular preaching and free distribution of essential commodities
explanation: The Muslim leaders contributed to the spread of Islam in West Africa through their moral standard and mode of worship which impressed the local people. Their lifestyle and adherence to the teachings of Islam served as a model for others to follow, thereby facilitating the spread of Islam.

topics.title: THE IMPACT OF ISLAM IN WEST AFRICA : THE INFLUENCE OF ISLAM ON THE SOCIO-POLITICAL LIFE OF SOME WEST AFRICAN EMPIRES: GHANA, MALI, SONGHAI AND BORNO

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge (IRK)
q: Shaykh 'Abdul-Qadir al-Jilani was the founder of
explanation: Shaykh 'Abdul-Qadir al-Jilani was the founder of the Qadiriyyah Sufi order. Sufi orders are spiritual paths in Islam, and the Qadiriyyah order is one of the oldest and most widespread, named after its founder Shaykh 'Abdul-Qadir al-Jilani.

topics.title: EARLY CONTACT OF ISLAM WITH AFRICA : THE ROLE OF TRADERS, TEACHERS, PREACHERS, MURABITUN, SUFI ORDERS AND MUJADDIDUN TO THE SPREAD OF ISLAM IN WEST AFRICA.

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge (IRK)
q: The main cause of the Battle of Uhud was that
options: the Quraysh wanted to revenge what befell them at the Battle of Badr
the Quraysh wanted to regain their lost territory
Muslims wanted to revenge what happened to them at the Battle of Badr
Abu Sufyan, the leader of the cravan wanted to become a king
explanation: The main cause of the Battle of Uhud was that the Quraysh wanted to avenge their defeat at the Battle of Badr. The Battle of Uhud was a significant battle in the early days of Islam and took place between the early Muslims and the Quraysh of Mecca.

topics.title: THE LIFE OF PROPHET MUHAMMAD (SAW) : THE BATTLES OF BADR, UHUD AND KHANDAQ: CAUSES AND EFFECTS

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge (IRK)
q: The famous Islamic scholar who was called the father of the science of history was
explanation: Ibn Khaldun is often recognized as a pioneer in the fields of historiography and sociology, and thus he is sometimes referred to as the father of these disciplines, or more specifically, the father of the philosophy of history.

topics.title: CONTRIBUTIONS OF ISLAM TO EDUCATION : THE LIVES AND CONTRIBUTIONS OF IBN SINA, AL-GHAZALI, IBN RUSHDI, AR-RAZIO AND IBN KHALDUN TO EDUCATION.

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge (IRK)
q: The Muslim scholar who championed rights and education of women in West Africa was
explanation: Usman dan Fodio was a religious teacher, writer and Islamic promoter, and the founder of the Sokoto Caliphate. Dan Fodio was one of the first Islamic leaders to advocate for women's education, arguing that women were just as capable as men of achieving spiritual understanding and should be educated.

topics.title: CONTRIBUTIONS OF ISLAM TO EDUCATION : INTELLECTUAL ACTIVITIES OF AHMAD BABA OF TIMBUKTU, SHEIKHAL-MAGHILI, SHEIKH USMAN DANFODIO, SULTAN MUHAMMAD BELLO AND IBN BATTUTA

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge (IRK)
q: In Islam, syncretism means
explanation: In Islam, syncretism generally refers to the mixing of Islamic beliefs with other beliefs, which can include practices from other religions, cultural traditions, or philosophical systems. This is often viewed negatively in Islam, as it can lead to deviations from the teachings of the Quran and the Prophet Muhammad.

topics.title: TAWHID AND FIQH : GENERAL PRACTICES WHICH ARE INCOMPATIBLE WITH ISLAMIC PRINCIPLES OF TAWHID

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge (IRK)
q: Which of the following books was written by Ibu Sina?
explanation: Ibn Sina, also known as Avicenna, was a Persian polymath who is regarded as one of the most significant physicians, astronomers, thinkers and writers of the Islamic Golden Age. He wrote many books on various subjects, including philosophy and medicine. His most famous work is 'The Canon of Medicine' (Al-Qanun fi at-Tibb), which was a standard medical text at many medieval universities and remained in use as late as 1650.

topics.title: CONTRIBUTIONS OF ISLAM TO EDUCATION : THE LIVES AND CONTRIBUTIONS OF IBN SINA, AL-GHAZALI, IBN RUSHDI, AR-RAZIO AND IBN KHALDUN TO EDUCATION.

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge (IRK)
q: The man who analyzed the first encounter of the Prophet (S.A.W.) with Angel Jibril was
explanation: Waraqah b. Nawfal was the man who analyzed the first encounter of the Prophet (S.A.W.) with Angel Jibril. He was a cousin of Khadijah (the Prophet's first wife) and a Christian monk who had knowledge of the previous scriptures. When the Prophet (S.A.W.) had his first encounter with Angel Jibril and received the first revelation, he was confused and scared. Khadijah took him to Waraqah who confirmed that the visitor was indeed the Angel Jibril and that Muhammad (S.A.W.) was the last Prophet.

topics.title: THE LIFE OF PROPHET MUHAMMAD (SAW) : HIS CALL TO PROPHETHOOD

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge (IRK)
q: The main message of the verse above is that the
options: Muslim 'Ummah should ensure that the Glorious Qur'an is not corrupted
protection of the Glorious Qur'an is a divine responsibility
protection of the Glorious Qur'an is a joint responsibility
Prophet (S.A.W.) has taken adequate measures to protect the Glorious Qur'an
explanation: The main message of the verse above is that the protection of the Glorious Qur'an is a divine responsibility.

topics.title: PROOF OF THE DIVINE AUTHENTICITY OF THE GLORIOUS QUR'AN : DIVINE PRESERVATION OF THE GLORIOUS QUR'AN

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge (IRK)
q: The verse above, is an expression of man's absolute
explanation: The verse is an expression of man's absolute loyalty to 'Allah.

topics.title: TAWHID AND FIQH : FAITH

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge (IRK)
q: In surah al-Qaricah, mankind is compared to
explanation: In Surah Al-Qari'ah (The Calamity), mankind is compared to scattered moths. This is a metaphor used in the Quran to describe the chaos and terror that will be prevalent on the Day of Judgment. The comparison of people to scattered moths illustrates the helplessness and disarray of mankind on that day.

topics.title: STUDY OF THE ARABIC TEXT OF THE FOLLOWING SUWAR/AYATS WITH TAJWID : AL -QARI'AH

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge (IRK)
q: Fount (of abundance) was given to the Prophet (S.A.W.) and made known to him in surah
explanation: The 'Fount of Abundance' was given to the Prophet (S.A.W.) and made known to him in Surah Al-Kawthar. Al-Kawthar is the shortest Surah in the Quran and it was revealed to the Prophet (S.A.W.) at a time when his enemies were rejoicing at his son's death. The Surah gave the Prophet (S.A.W.) the glad tidings of Al-Kawthar, which is a river in Paradise and also signifies abundance.

topics.title: STUDY OF THE ARABIC TEXT OF THE FOLLOWING SUWAR/AYATS WITH TAJWID : AL -KAWTHAR

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge (IRK)
q: The verse above is immediately followed by
explanation:
The verse that follows it is:
إِنَّ هَٰذَا لَفِي ٱلصُّحُفِ ٱلْأُولَىٰ
Translation: "Indeed, this is in the former scriptures."
— Surah Al-A'la (87:18)

topics.title: STUDY OF THE ARABIC TEXT OF THE FOLLOWING SUWAR/AYATS WITH TAJWID : AL -FALAQ

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge (IRK)
q: The basic theme of surah al-Zil Zal is the description of
options: fearful events of the Day of Judgement
how people will be rewarded with Paradise
explanation: The basic theme of Surah Al-Zilzal (The Earthquake) is the description of the fearful events of the Day of Judgment. The Surah vividly describes the chaos and terror of the Day of Judgment when the earth will shake its final earthquake, and it will throw out its burdens (the dead), and man will say: 'What is the matter with it?' On that day, it will report its news because your Lord will have inspired it.

topics.title: TAFSIR : IMPORTANCE AND TYPES OF TAFSIR

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge (IRK)
q: The major theme of ayah Amanar- Rasul is
explanation: Ayah Amanar-Rasul is a verse in the Quran that outlines the articles of faith in Islam. These include belief in Allah, His angels, His books, His messengers, the Day of Judgment, and predestination. Therefore, the major theme of Ayah Amanar-Rasul is the articles of faith.

topics.title: ARTICLE OF FAITH : BELIEF IN ALLAH

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge (IRK)
q: The Sahaba were the first to embark on the interpretation of the Glorious Qur'an in order to
options: bring out the treasures in the Glorious Qur'an
get used to the language of the Glorious Qur'an
explanation: The Sahaba, who were the companions of the Prophet Muhammad, were the first to embark on the interpretation of the Quran. Their main aim was to understand and bring out the treasures in the Quran, not to be known as teachers of Tafsir or to excel above other scholars. They wanted to understand the teachings of the Quran and apply them in their lives.

topics.title: TAFSIR : HISTORICAL DEVELOPMENT OF TAFSIR

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge (IRK)
q: The scholar who adopted Tafsir bil Ma'athur in his commentary was
explanation: Tafsir bil Ma'thur refers to the interpretation of the Quran based on transmitted reports, including sayings of the Prophet Muhammad and his companions. At-Tabari Abu Jafar Muhammad b. Jarir was a renowned scholar who adopted this method in his commentary of the Quran.

topics.title: TAFSIR : HISTORICAL DEVELOPMENT OF TAFSIR

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge (IRK)
q: The term 'Asma ar-Rijal' refers to the investigation of the
explanation: 'Asma ar-Rijal' is a discipline in Hadith studies where the credibility of the narrators of Hadith is investigated. This is done to ensure the authenticity of the Hadith. Therefore, 'Asma ar-Rijal' refers to the criticism of the narrators.

topics.title: HADITH : AUTHENTICATION OF HADITH

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge (IRK)
q: The importance of knowledge of isnad and matn lies in the fact that
options: the two make it easy to memorize Hadith
the two make it easy to identify fault in Hadith
they make it possible to understand Hadith
explanation: Isnad refers to the chain of narrators of a Hadith, while Matn is the content of the Hadith. Knowledge of both is important in Hadith studies as it makes it easy to identify any faults in the Hadith. This is crucial in ensuring the authenticity of the Hadith.

topics.title: HADITH : AUTHENTICATION OF HADITH

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge (IRK)
q: The term al-Mutawatir refers to Hadith narrated by
explanation: The term al-Mutawatir refers to Hadiths that have been narrated by a large number of narrators at each level of their chain of transmission, providing a very high level of authenticity and certainty. Therefore, the correct answer is 'large number of narrators'.

topics.title: HADITH : AUTHENTICATION OF HADITH

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge (IRK)
q: The Prophet (S.A.W.) enjoins that if anyone sees an abomination, the least thing to do is to
explanation: According to the Hadith, if anyone sees an abomination, the least thing to do is to hate it in his heart. This is the lowest level of faith. The other levels include speaking out against it or changing it with his hands, but these are not options in the question.

topics.title: MORAL LESSONS IN THE GLORIOUS QUR'AN AND HADITH : ENJOINING WHAT IS GOOD AND FORBIDDING WHAT IS WRONG HADITH 25 AND 34 OF AN NAWAWI

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge (IRK)
q: According to Hadith 31 of an-Nawawi, man will be loved by 'Allah if he is
explanation: According to Hadith 31 of an-Nawawi, one of the ways to be loved by Allah is to be indifferent to the world. This means to not be overly attached to worldly possessions and pleasures, and to focus on spiritual growth and the hereafter.

topics.title: HADITH : THE STUDY OF THE ARABIC TEXTS OF THE FOLLOWING AHADITH FROMAN-NAWAWI’S COLLECTION: 1,3,5,6,7,9,10,11,12,13,15,16, 18,19,21,22,25,27,34, AND 41

q: The statement in Hadith 25 above gave rise to the explanation as to the different ways by which
explanation: The statement in Hadith 25 gave rise to the explanation as to the different ways by which alms can be given.

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge (IRK)
q: In surah 17:23 'Allah decrees that
options: believers should not say what they will not do
prayers should be observed at their appointed times
no distinction should be made between the prophets
explanation: In Surah 17:23, Allah decrees that none should be worshipped but Him. This is the fundamental principle of Tawhid, the oneness of God, which is the cornerstone of Islamic belief.

topics.title: ARTICLE OF FAITH : BELIEF IN ALLAH

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge (IRK)
q: Labour crises could be resolved if employers of labour adhere to the Prophet's advice which says
options: pay the labourer before his sweat is dry
it is better to cut firewood and sell than to beg
the honour of a Muslim should not be violated
explanation: The Prophet's advice that could help resolve labour crises is to 'pay the labourer before his sweat is dry'. This means that workers should be paid promptly and fairly for their work, which would help to prevent disputes and dissatisfaction.

topics.title: MORAL LESSONS IN THE GLORIOUS QUR’AN AND HADITH : DIGNITY OF LABOUR (Q.62:10) (Q.78:11) HADITH FROM BUKHARI AND IBNMAJAH: “THAT ONE OF YOU TAKES HIS ROPE…….” “NEVER HAS ANYONE OF YOU EATEN…..”.

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge (IRK)
q: Q. 2:219 prohibits wine and gambling for the muslims because
options: the sins in them are greater than the profits
they lead one to ignore one's religion
explanation: Surah 2:219 of the Quran prohibits Muslims from consuming wine and engaging in gambling because the sins or harm resulting from them are greater than any potential benefits or profits. This is based on the principle of weighing the benefits and harms in Islamic jurisprudence.

topics.title: MORAL LESSONS IN THE GLORIOUS QUR’AN AND HADITH : PROHIBITION OF BRIBERY AND CORRUPTION (Q:2:188), ALCOHOL AND GAMBLING (Q.2:219) (Q.5:93-94), STEALING AND FRAUD (Q.5:41) (83:1-5),SMOKING, DRUG ABUSE AND OTHER INTOXICANTS (Q.2:172-173, 195 AND 219) (Q.4:43) (Q.5:3) (Q.6:118-121) ARROGANCE (Q.31:18-19) AND EXTRAVAGANCE (Q.17:26-27) (Q.31:18-19)

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge (IRK)
q: The forbidden type of food listed in surah 2:173 include carrion, blood, swine flesh and that which is
explanation: Surah 2:173 of the Quran lists the types of food that are forbidden for Muslims to consume. These include carrion (dead animals), blood, swine flesh (pork), and anything that is dedicated to other than Allah (God). This is because Islam emphasizes the concept of 'Tayyib', which means 'good and pure', and these foods are considered impure or harmful.

topics.title: IBADAT AND THEIR TYPES : TAHARAH, ITS TYPES AND IMPORTANCE AL-ISTINJA'/ISTIJMAR, ALWUDU', AT-TAYAMMUM AND AL-GHUSL HADITH 10 AND 23 OF AN-NAWAWI

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge (IRK)
q: The admonition against arrogance in surah 31:18 is directed to the son of
explanation: The admonition against arrogance in Surah 31:18 is directed to the son of Luqman. Luqman was a wise man in Islamic tradition who gave his son various pieces of advice, including the warning against arrogance.

topics.title: MORAL LESSONS IN THE GLORIOUS QUR’AN AND HADITH : ARROGANCE (Q.31:18-19) AND EXTRAVAGANCE (Q.17:26-27) (Q.31:18-19)

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge (IRK)
q:
'... here are my daughters: they are purer for you ...' (Q. 11: 78)
The statement above was made by Prophet Lut to discourage
explanation: The statement '... here are my daughters: they are purer for you ...' (Q. 11: 78) was made by Prophet Lut to discourage homosexuality. In the story, the people of Lut were engaging in homosexual behavior, and Lut offered his daughters in marriage to them as a lawful alternative.

explanation: Surah 2:195 can be interpreted as discouraging smoking.

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge (IRK)
q: Mutilation which is stipulated in Q. 5:38 for theft is described as
explanation: The punishment for theft as stipulated in Q. 5:38 is described as an exemplary punishment. This means it is intended to serve as a deterrent to others. The verse states that a thief, male or female, should have their hand cut off as a recompense for what they committed, as an exemplary punishment from Allah.

topics.title: MORAL LESSONS IN THE GLORIOUS QUR’AN AND HADITH : PROHIBITION OF BRIBERY AND CORRUPTION (Q:2:188), ALCOHOL AND GAMBLING (Q.2:219) (Q.5:93-94), STEALING AND FRAUD (Q.5:41) (83:1-5),SMOKING, DRUG ABUSE AND OTHER INTOXICANTS (Q.2:172-173, 195 AND 219) (Q.4:43) (Q.5:3) (Q.6:118-121) ARROGANCE (Q.31:18-19) AND EXTRAVAGANCE (Q.17:26-27) (Q.31:18-19)

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge (IRK)
q:
'Allah doth command you
To render back your Trusts
To those to whom they are due;
And when ye judge
Between mand and man
That ye judge with justice ...' (Q. 4: 58)
Inherent in the Qur'anic verse above are the virtues of amanah and
explanation: The verse quoted in the question is from the Quran (4:58) and it emphasizes the virtues of trustworthiness (amanah) and justice (adalah). The verse instructs to return trusts to their rightful owners and to judge with justice when arbitrating between people. Therefore, the correct answer is 'adalah' which means justice in Arabic.

topics.title: MORAL LESSONS IN THE GLORIOUS QUR’AN AND HADITH : TRUST AND OBLIGATIONS (Q:4:58) (Q.5:1) AND PROMISES (Q.16:91) HADITH ‘HE HAS (REALLY) NO FAITH ….. NOT FULFILLED HIS PROMISE” (BAIHAQI)

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge (IRK)
q: Ar-Rahman as one of the attributes of Allah implies that He is
explanation: Ar-Rahman is one of the 99 names of Allah in Islam, and it means 'The Most Merciful'. This attribute of Allah implies His immense kindness and mercy towards His servants. Therefore, the correct answer is 'kind to His servants'.

topics.title: ARTICLE OF FAITH : BELIEF IN ALLAH

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge (IRK)
q: The word Hafizin in Q. 82:10 refers to
explanation: The word 'Hafizin' in Q. 82:10 refers to 'Guardians' or 'Protectors'. In the context of the Quran, these guardians are often interpreted as angels who record the deeds of humans. Therefore, the correct answer is 'Angels'.

topics.title: ARTICLE OF FAITH : BELIEF IN ALLAH'S ANGELS

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge (IRK)
q: The prophet who assisted Prophet Ibrahim (A.S.) in the reconstruction of the Kacabah was
explanation: The Quran mentions that Prophet Ibrahim (A.S.) and his son Prophet Ismail (A.S.) were the ones who reconstructed the Ka'abah. Therefore, the correct answer is 'Isma'il (A.S.)'.

topics.title: THE LIFE OF PROPHET MUHAMMAD (SAW) : HIS BIRTH AND EARLY LIFE

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge (IRK)
q: Surah 79:24 reveals that Pharaoh's action incompatible with the Islamic principle of Tawhid because he
explanation: Surah 79:24 of the Quran reveals that Pharaoh declared 'I am your supreme lord'. This action is incompatible with the Islamic principle of Tawhid (the oneness of God) because he equated himself with Allah. Therefore, the correct answer is 'equaled himself with 'Allah'.

topics.title: TAWHID AND FIQH : GENERAL PRACTICES WHICH ARE INCOMPATIBLE WITH ISLAMIC PRINCIPLES OF TAWHID

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge (IRK)
q: The art of witchcraft is disapproved of in surah
explanation: The art of witchcraft is disapproved of in Surah Al-Falaq. This Surah is a plea to Allah for protection from various evils, including witchcraft.

topics.title: TAWHID AND FIQH : SHIRK

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge (IRK)
q: A mature Muslim who wakes up only to discover that it was the first day of Ramadan is expected to
explanation: A mature Muslim who wakes up only to discover that it was the first day of Ramadan is expected to observe imsak and make up. Imsak means abstaining from things that invalidate the fast from dawn until sunset. The person is expected to make up for the part of the day they missed fasting.

topics.title: FAMILY MATTERS : MARRIAGE

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge (IRK)
q: Zakatul-fitr is not permissible on
explanation: Zakatul-fitr is not permissible on kolanut. Zakatul-fitr is a form of charity given to the poor at the end of Ramadan. It can be given in the form of staple food items like rice, maize, and millet, but not kolanut.

topics.title: IBADAT AND THEIR TYPES : ZAKAH

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge (IRK)
q: Jihad against carnal self is a
explanation: Jihad against carnal self is a major jihad. Jihad means struggle or striving. The major jihad is the inner spiritual struggle of the self against evil tendencies and desires.

topics.title: IBADAT AND THEIR TYPES : JIHAD

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge (IRK)
q:
' .. . He created for you mates from among yourselves, that ye may dwell in tranquility with them...' (Q. 30: 21)
The verse above discusses the
explanation: The verse 'He created for you mates from among yourselves, that ye may dwell in tranquility with them...' (Q. 30: 21) discusses the reasons for marriage. It highlights the purpose of marriage as a means for spouses to live in tranquility with each other.

topics.title: FAMILY MATTERS : MARRIAGE

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge (IRK)
q: The message contained in the Glorious Qur'an 4:34 centres on the
options: role of a man in the family
explanation: The message contained in the Glorious Qur'an 4:34 centres on the rights and duties of a couple.

topics.title: FAMILY MATTERS : MARRIAGE

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge (IRK)
q: Men and women are considered equal in Q. 16:97 in relation to
explanation: Men and women are considered equal in Q. 16:97 in relation to piety.

topics.title: ARTICLE OF FAITH : BELIEF IN ALLAH

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge (IRK)
q: Where the husband and wife mutually agree to terminate their marriage contract without anything against each other, such a dissolution is termed
explanation: Mubara'ah is a mutual divorce where both husband and wife agree to terminate their marriage contract without any ill feelings towards each other. Ila, Faskh, and Khulc are other forms of divorce in Islamic law, but they do not specifically refer to a mutual agreement without anything against each other.

topics.title: FAMILY MATTERS : DIVORCE

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge (IRK)
q: The period of custody of a baby girl after her mother has been divorced lasts until she is
explanation: In Islamic law, the period of custody of a baby girl after her mother has been divorced lasts until she is married. This is because the responsibility of a woman's protection and maintenance in Islam is on her male relatives, in this case, her husband.

topics.title: FAMILY MATTERS : MARRIAGE

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge (IRK)
q: In the Islamic law of inheritance, the share of the mother of the deceased who has no children is
explanation: In the Islamic law of inheritance, the share of the mother of the deceased who has no children is 1/3.

topics.title: FAMILY MATTERS : INHERITANCE

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge (IRK)
q: Fard and Haram in the scope of sharicah mean
explanation: In the context of Islamic Shari'ah, 'Fard' refers to an act that is obligatory for Muslims, while 'Haram' refers to anything that is explicitly prohibited by the Quran. Therefore, 'Fard and Haram' mean 'obligatory and prohibited'.

topics.title: TAWHID AND FIQH : FAITH

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge (IRK)
q:
'If ye do it not, take notice of war from 'Allah and His Apostle: But if ye turn back, ye shall have your capital summs . . .'
(Q. 2: 279)
The quotation above warns against
explanation: The quoted verse (Q. 2: 279) is a warning against usury, also known as interest or 'riba' in Arabic. In Islam, usury is strictly prohibited as it is considered exploitative.

topics.title: ISLAMIC ECONOMIC SYSTEM : ISLAMIC ATTITUDE TO RIBA HADITH 6TH OF AN-NAWAWI

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge (IRK)
q: Who among the prophets of 'Allah was sent to his people to warn against deceit in terms of measurement and weight?
explanation: Prophet Shuaib (A.S.) was sent to his people, the Midianites, to warn them against deceit in terms of measurement and weight. He preached against dishonesty in business transactions, which was a common practice among his people.

topics.title: ARTICLE OF FAITH : HIS PROPHETS: ULUL-AZMI

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge (IRK)
q: The worst vice prevalent during the Jahiliyyah period was
explanation: The worst vice prevalent during the Jahiliyyah period was disloyality.

topics.title: ISLAMIC HISTORY AND CIVILIZATION - PRE-ISLAMIC ARABIA JAHILIYYAH : JAHILIYYAH PRACTICES: IDOL WORSHIP, INFANTICIDE, POLYANDRY, GAMBLING, USURY, ETC.

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge (IRK)
q: The Jahiliyyah practice that was gradually phased out is
explanation: The Jahiliyyah practice that was gradually phased out is alcoholism.

topics.title: ISLAMIC HISTORY AND CIVILIZATION - PRE-ISLAMIC ARABIA JAHILIYYAH : ISLAMIC REFORMS

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge (IRK)
q: One of the most outstanding developments after the Hijrah was the
explanation: After the Hijrah, which is the migration of Prophet Muhammad and his followers from Mecca to Medina, one of the most significant developments was the emergence of the Muslim 'Ummah. The term 'Ummah' refers to the collective community of Islamic peoples, which is bound together by ties of religion. In Medina, Prophet Muhammad established a Muslim community and set up a constitution known as the Constitution of Medina which regulated the rights and responsibilities of all citizens and the relationship between the different communities in the city.

topics.title: THE LIFE OF PROPHET MUHAMMAD (SAW) : THE HIJRAH

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge (IRK)
q: The Makkan forces were not successful at the battle of Khandaq because they
options: were not familiar with the strategy of the Muslims
explanation: The Battle of Khandaq, also known as the Battle of the Trench, was a defensive battle fought by the Muslims led by Prophet Muhammad against the Quraysh of Mecca and their allies. The Makkan forces were not successful because they were not familiar with the strategy of the Muslims. The Muslims had dug a trench around Medina, which was a novel strategy in Arabian warfare and this prevented the Makkan cavalry from breaking through.

topics.title: THE LIFE OF PROPHET MUHAMMAD (SAW) : THE BATTLES OF BADR, UHUD AND KHANDAQ: CAUSES AND EFFECTS

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge (IRK)
q: Who among the four rightly guided caliphs introduced the systems of Bayt al mal?
explanation: The system of Bayt al-Mal, which is an Arabic term that means 'House of Wealth' and refers to the financial treasury and state treasury in the early Islamic Caliphates, was introduced by Umar b. al-Khattab, the second of the four rightly guided caliphs. He established this system to manage the wealth and the expenditures of the state.

topics.title: THE RIGHTLY GUIDED CALIPHS AL-KHULAFA'U RASHIDUN : THE LIVES AND CONTRIBUTIONS OF THE FOUR RIGHTLY GUIDED CALIPHS

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge (IRK)
q: The Muslim soldier who introduced Islam into Tripoli was
explanation: Uqbah b. Nafi is credited with the introduction of Islam into Tripoli. He was a general in the army of the Umayyad Caliphate and led the Islamic conquest of the Maghreb (North Africa).

topics.title: EARLY CONTACT OF ISLAM WITH AFRICA : THE SPREAD OF ISLAM TO EGYPT

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge (IRK)
q: One of the factors which helped the spread of Islam in West Africa was
options: the building of a mosque in Timbuktu
explanation: One of the factors which helped the spread of Islam in West Africa was effective trade relationships. Muslim traders from North Africa and the Middle East travelled to West Africa for trade, and they brought with them the Islamic religion. The interaction between these Muslim traders and the local West African people led to the spread of Islam in the region.

topics.title: THE IMPACT OF ISLAM IN WEST AFRICA : THE INFLUENCE OF ISLAM ON THE SOCIO-POLITICAL LIFE OF SOME WEST AFRICAN EMPIRES: GHANA, MALI, SONGHAI AND BORNO

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge (IRK)
q: The first man on earth, Adam (A. S.). was exalted above other creatures because of his
explanation: The first man on earth, Adam (A.S.), was exalted above other creatures because of his knowledge.

topics.title: ARTICLE OF FAITH : BELIEF IN ALLAH

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge (IRK)
q: Wahy in relation to the Glorious Qu'an means
explanation: Waḥy is the Arabic word for revelation. In Islamic belief, revelations are God's Word delivered by his chosen individuals – known as Messengers prophets – to mankind.

topics.title: THE QUR'AN AND HADITH - REVELATION OF THE GLORIOUS QUR'AN : DIFFERENT MODES OF REVELATION: INSPIRATION BEHIND THE VEIL, THROUGH AN ANGEL, ETC.

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge (IRK)
q: The Makkan Suwar lay emphasis on oneness of Allah because
options: it was the only way to call Makkans to Islam
It is the only article of faith
it is the basis of Iman and other articles of faith
explanation: The Makkan Suwar lay emphasis on the oneness of Allah because the pre-Islamic Makkans worshipped many gods. The Makkan period of the Prophet's mission was characterized by the struggle to establish the belief in the oneness of Allah (Tawhid) in a society that was steeped in polytheism.

topics.title: ARTICLE OF FAITH : BELIEF IN ALLAH

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge (IRK)
q: The uniqueness of the Glorious Qur'an, according to Q.39:28, lies in the fact that it is
explanation: According to Q.39:28, the uniqueness of the Glorious Qur'an lies in the fact that it is revealed in Arabic. The verse says, 'An Arabic Qur'an, without any deviance that they might become righteous.' The Arabic language is known for its richness and depth, which makes the Qur'an's message clear and precise.

topics.title: PROOF OF THE DIVINE AUTHENTICITY OF THE GLORIOUS QUR'AN : UNIQUENESS OF THE GLORIOUS QUR'AN

q: One of the sahabah considered most knowledgeable in Tafsir was
explanation: Abdullah b. Abbas was an uncle to the prophet. The prophet prayed for him at birth "O" Allah give him understanding of the religion and teach him the knowledge of the explanation of Quran".

q: The contents of Surah al-fatihah can be summed up to mean that
options: man must always fear Allah and obey Him
man must worship Allah and seek His help
It is the first surah that was revealed
explanation:
Suratu Fatihah is otherwise known as Ummul kitab or Sab'ul mathemin. It is subdivided into three parts:
1 - Glorifications and attributes of Allah
2 - Government between the servant and his lord
3 - prayer for guardance

topics.title: STUDY OF THE ARABIC TEXT OF THE FOLLOWING SUWAR/AYATS WITH TAJWID : AL-FATIHAH

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge (IRK)
q: A text of Hadith is rejected if it is
explanation: A Hadith is considered Sahih (authentic) if it has a reliable chain of narrators, and the content does not contradict the Quran or other Hadiths. If a Hadith contradicts natural phenomena or established facts, it is likely to be rejected because Islam does not contradict logic or established facts. Therefore, a Hadith is rejected if it is 'against natural phenomenon'.

explanation: The Muwatta Malik is a collection of Hadiths compiled by Imam Malik. It is unique in that it also includes the practices of the people of Madinah, making it a valuable source of information about the early Islamic community. Therefore, the correct answer is 'Muwatta Malik'.

explanation: Surah At-Tin (The Fig) is the 95th Surah of the Quran. In this Surah, Allah swears by the fig and the olive, Mount Sinai, and the city of security (Makkah), and then mentions the creation of man in the best of stature (orderly proportion).

explanation: The Quran mentions the stages of the growth of the human embryo in several verses, which were confirmed by modern embryology. This is one of the miracles of the Quran that proves its authenticity.

explanation: In Islamic law, 'Al-awrah' refers to the parts of the body that are required to be covered in public. For men, this is generally considered to be from the navel to the knee.

explanation: Quran 24:30 instructs Muslim men to lower their gaze, which means to avoid looking at things that are inappropriate or could lead to sinful thoughts or actions.

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge (IRK)
q: According to the Prophet (S.A.W), he who betrays trust is regarded as having
explanation: According to the Prophet Muhammad (S.A.W), he who betrays trust is regarded as having neither faith nor religion. Trust is a fundamental value in Islam, and betraying it is considered a serious sin.

topics.title: HADITH : THE RELATIONSHIP BETWEEN HADITH AND THE GLORIOUS QUR’AN

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge (IRK)
q: Allah's existence is often demostrated in the glorious Qur-an by reference to his
explanation: In the Qur'an, Allah's existence is often demonstrated by reference to His creation. This is because the creation of the universe and everything within it is seen as a testament to Allah's power and existence.

topics.title: ARTICLE OF FAITH : BELIEF IN ALLAH

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge (IRK)
q: The messenger of Allah sent to the people of Thamud was
explanation: The messenger of Allah sent to the people of Thamud was Prophet Salih (A.S). He was sent to guide the people of Thamud who had deviated from the path of Allah.

topics.title: ARTICLE OF FAITH : HIS PROPHETS: ULUL-AZMI

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge (IRK)
q: One of the revealed books Allah promised to protect against distortion is
explanation: Al-Furqan is one of the revealed books that Allah promised to protect against distortion. Al-Furqan is another name for the Qur'an, which is believed by Muslims to be the word of Allah as revealed to Prophet Muhammad.

topics.title: ARTICLE OF FAITH : HIS BOOKS

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge (IRK)
q: Yawmuddin refers to the day of
explanation: Yawmuddin refers to the Day of Judgement. It is the day when all beings will be held accountable for their actions in this life.

explanation: Ibadar (worship) and Muamal (Interpersonal relations) are part of scope of Figla. Figh means understanding of legal ruling in Islam

topics.title: TAWHID AND FIQH : FAITH

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge (IRK)
q: Taking the ritual bath for Friday prayers is
explanation: Taking the ritual bath for Friday prayers is considered Sunnah in Islam. Sunnah refers to the teachings, deeds, and sayings of the Prophet Muhammad (PBUH) that are recommended to follow but are not obligatory (wajib).

topics.title: IBADAT AND THEIR TYPES : HAJJ

q: The number of Takbir observed during Salah al-Janazah is
explanation: Salaf-ul Janazah, a funeral prayer. It is Fard kifayah. It consist of four Rak'ah with neithr Rulauh nor Sujud.

explanation: Jamrat-al-Aqabah is a symbolize of act during haji where pilgrims throw 7 pepples on the 10th day of Dhul Hijjah.

topics.title: IBADAT AND THEIR TYPES : HAJJ

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge (IRK)
q: The next place for a muslim to spend the night after 'Arafat' is
explanation: Muzdalifah is a place where pilgrims are expected to spend their night after Arafah. Periodic fasting for self discipline would automatically prevent such person from vices (Zinah).

topics.title: IBADAT AND THEIR TYPES : HAJJ

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge (IRK)
q: A muslim who cannot marry for genuine reasons is advised to
options: be reciting the Glorious Qur-an for spiritual uplift
give sadaqah to the poor and needy
explanation: A Muslim who cannot marry for genuine reasons is advised to fast periodically for self-discipline. Fasting is seen as a means of controlling one's desires and can be a way to prevent illicit sexual activity. This is based on the Hadith of Prophet Muhammad (PBUH) where he said, 'O young men, whoever among you can afford to get married, let him do so, and whoever cannot afford it, let him fast, for it will be a shield for him.'

topics.title: FAMILY MATTERS : MARRIAGE

explanation: Idrar means iII-treatment of wife Allah. "Lodge them (the divorce women) where you dwell, according to your mean and do not harm them".Q65:6

explanation:

topics.title: FAMILY MATTERS : DIVORCE

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge (IRK)
q: A woman who loses her husband while undergoing an 'iddah of revocable divorce changes to 'iddah
explanation: In Islam, a woman who loses her husband while undergoing an 'iddah of revocable divorce changes to 'iddah al-wafat. 'Iddah' is a period of waiting that a woman must observe after the death of her spouse or after a divorce, during which she may not marry another man. 'Al-wafat' refers to the 'iddah' observed after the death of a spouse. The other options do not accurately describe this transition.

topics.title: FAMILY MATTERS : DIVORCE

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge (IRK)
q: Where the father is the only one heir who survives his son, he will inherit
explanation: Father has his portion in inheritance it could be 1/6 if there is a child but if there is no child he would take 1/3 so if there is nobody to inherit, the father would inherit the whole of the property

topics.title: FAMILY MATTERS : INHERITANCE

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge (IRK)
q: In an Islamic state, the right to pardon a murderer lies with the
explanation: In an Islamic state, the right to pardon a murderer lies with the deceased's relatives. This is based on the Islamic principle of Qisas, which allows the family of the victim to decide whether to demand the death penalty or to pardon the murderer, often in exchange for Diyya (blood money).

explanation: Ibn Rushd, also known as Averroes, was a renowned philosopher and also a jurist. He made significant contributions to Islamic jurisprudence and philosophy. He is known for his commentaries on Aristotle's works and for his work in law and theology.

explanation: The complete Glorious Qur'an was taken from Lawn al-mahfuz to the lowest of the heavens known as Bait al- Izzah. Lawn al-mahfuz, also known as the Preserved Tablet, is the divine record that contains all that has happened and will happen. Bait al- Izzah, also known as the House of Honor, is believed to be the location in the lowest heaven where the Qur'an was first brought down.

guiding the new converts in Islam ways of life
explanation: The Sah'bah, the companions of the Prophet Muhammad, played a crucial role in preserving the Glorious Qur'an. One of the most important ways they did this was by establishing schools in major cities and teaching the Glorious Qur'an. This helped to ensure that the Qur'an was accurately transmitted to future generations.

q: A Hadith in which the chain of narration and text are sound is known as
explanation: A Hadith in which the chain of narration and text are sound is known as Sahih. Sahih Hadiths are considered the most authentic and reliable Hadiths in Islamic tradition. They are characterized by a continuous and reliable chain of narrators, the integrity and strong memory of the narrators, and the Hadith text does not contradict the Quran or other Sahih Hadiths.

explanation: Hadith Daif means weak which can be used on certain conditions. The weakness of Hadith could be through chains of transmittors, poor memory of the reporters and questionable character of the reporter

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge (IRK)
q: Hadith is important to Muslims because it is
options: the most reliable sayings of the prophet (SAW)
the only linkage between the prophet(SAW) and the Muslim
the means through which the prophet's message is known
explanation: Hadith is important to Muslims because it is the means through which the prophet's message is known. Hadiths provide context to the verses of the Qur'an and offer guidance on how to live according to Islamic principles. They are a crucial source of Islamic law and belief, supplementing the Qur'an by providing detailed accounts of the Prophet's life, his sayings, actions, and tacit approvals.

topics.title: HADITH : AUTHENTICATION OF HADITH

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge (IRK)
q: Which of the following is the correct order of authenticity of three of the six sound collections of the Hadith
explanation: The Hadiths are collections of the sayings, actions, and approvals of the Prophet Muhammad. They are ranked in terms of authenticity. The most authentic collections are Sahih Bukhari and Sahih Muslim. These are followed by the collections of Tirmidhi, Abu Dawud, Nasa'i, and Ibn Majah. Therefore, the correct order of authenticity is Bukhari, Muslim, and Tirmidhi.

explanation: The first major written work of a hadith was Muwatta Malik. It was compiled by Imam Malik ibn Anas and is considered to be from the earliest written collections of hadith literature in the history of Islam.

make takbiratul ihram and join the Imam
explanation: In congregational Salah, a person who comes late is expected to make takbiratul ihram and join the Imam. Takbiratul Ihram is the first 'Allahu Akbar' that initiates the prayer. It is a requirement for the person to do this before joining the prayer.

explanation: In Islam, a marriage is not valid without at least two witnesses. This is to ensure the legality and transparency of the marriage contract. The presence of witnesses helps to prevent disputes and misunderstandings in the future.

topics.title: FAMILY MATTERS : MARRIAGE

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge (IRK)
q: The right of a husband over his wife excludes
explanation: The right of a husband over his wife excludes total controlling the wife's property. In Islam, a woman has the right to own, manage, and dispose of her property without interference from her husband or anyone else.

explanation: Talaq al bai'in technically conveys the idea of an irrevocable divorce. This is a type of divorce in Islam where the husband has no right to take back his wife unless a new marriage contract is agreed upon.

explanation: The authenticity of the Glorious Qur'an has been proven by empirical evidence of stages of growth of human embryo. subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge (IRK)
q: One major peculiarity between the Makkan and Madinan Suwar of the Glorious Qur'an is that
options: Makkan Suwar are historical while Madinan Suwar are theoretical
Makkan Suwar are meant for daily rituals while Madinan Suwar are for commercial transactions
Makkan Suwar are purely to people of Makkan while the Madinan Suwar addresses the people of Madina
Makkan Suwar are short and poetic while the Madinan Suwar are longer and generic
explanation: One major peculiarity between the Makkan and Madinan Suwar of the Glorious Qur'an is that Makkan Suwar are short and poetic while the Madinan Suwar are longer and generic. The Makkan Suwar, revealed in Makkah, are generally shorter and more poetic, focusing on monotheism, morality, and the afterlife. The Madinan Suwar, revealed in Madinah, are generally longer and deal with social legislation and the Muslim community.

topics.title: PRESERVATION OF THE GLORIOUS QUR’AN : DIFFERENCES BETWEEN MAKKAH AND MADINAN SUWAR

explanation: The copy of the Glorious Qur'an in use was standardized during the period of Caliph Uthman b. Affan. During his caliphate, Uthman ordered the standardization of the Qur'anic text into the form used today, known as the 'Uthmanic codex'.

explanation: A Hadith is considered rejected if it contradicts established principles of the Quran or known facts, such as natural phenomena. This is because the Hadith, sayings and actions of Prophet Muhammad, are meant to align with the teachings of the Quran and reality. Therefore, if a Hadith is against natural phenomena, it is likely to be rejected.

explanation: The Muwatta Malik is a collection of Hadith compiled by Imam Malik. It is unique in that it includes not only Hadith of the Prophet Muhammad, but also the practices of the people of Madinah, providing a comprehensive view of the Islamic practices of the time.

are
explanation: In Hadith 6 of An-Nawawi's collection, Prophet Muhammad (SAW) said that the lawful (halal) and unlawful (haram) are clear, but between them are ambiguous matters. These are matters that are not clearly defined as either lawful or unlawful.

topics.title: HADITH : THE STUDY OF THE ARABIC TEXTS OF THE FOLLOWING AHADITH FROMAN-NAWAWI’S COLLECTION: 1,3,5,6,7,9,10,11,12,13,15,16, 18,19,21,22,25,27,34, AND 41

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge (IRK)
q:
"Indeed the people before you were destroyed by asking a lot of unnecessary questions".
The quotation above is in Hadith 9 of An- Nawawi which refers to the
explanation: The quotation is referring to the 'People of the Book', a term used in the Quran to refer to followers of previous scriptures, mainly Jews and Christians. The Hadith warns against excessive questioning and disputing about religious matters, which was a characteristic attributed to these groups.

topics.title: HADITH : THE STUDY OF THE ARABIC TEXTS OF THE FOLLOWING AHADITH FROMAN-NAWAWI’S COLLECTION: 1,3,5,6,7,9,10,11,12,13,15,16, 18,19,21,22,25,27,34, AND 41

explanation: Imam Tirmidhi, one of the six compilers of Hadith, became blind before his death. This is a historical fact known in Islamic studies.

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge (IRK)
q: In Q.17:27, the extravagant are likened to associates of
explanation: In Quran 17:27, the extravagant are likened to associates of Satan. The verse says, 'Indeed, the wasteful are brothers of the devils, and ever has Satan been to his Lord ungrateful.'

topics.title: MORAL LESSONS IN THE GLORIOUS QUR’AN AND HADITH : EXTRAVAGANCE (Q.17:26-27) (Q.31:18-19)

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge (IRK)
q: Islam decreed against begging through encouraging
explanation: Islam discourages begging and encourages the dignity of labour. This is because Islam promotes self-reliance, hard work, and earning a living through lawful means.

"Allah begets not nor is He begotten."
The quotation above condemns
explanation: The quotation 'Allah begets not nor is He begotten' is a verse from the Quran (112:3) that condemns the Christian doctrine of trinity, which posits that God is three in one (Father, Son, and Holy Spirit) and that Jesus is the begotten son of God.

topics.title: TAWHID AND FIQH : SHIRK

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge (IRK)
q: What is expected of a believing Muslim when an important decision needs to be made?
explanation: When a Muslim needs to take a decision, he should do Istikharah (divine consultation with Allah) through prayer.

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge (IRK)
q: Qadr to the majority of scholars in Islam means
explanation: Qadr in Islam refers to the concept of divine destiny. It is the belief that everything that happens in life, from good to bad, is preordained by Allah. Therefore, the correct answer is 'destiny'.

topics.title: ARTICLE OF FAITH : DESTINY: DISTINCTION BETWEEN QADA AND QADAR

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge (IRK)
q: The best Ibadah according to the Prophet (SAW) after the major pillars is to
explanation: According to Hadiths, the Prophet Muhammad (SAW) emphasized the importance of making a fellow Muslim happy. This act is considered a significant form of Ibadah (worship) after the major pillars of Islam. Therefore, the correct answer is 'make a fellow Muslim happy'.

topics.title: IBADAT AND THEIR TYPES : GOOD DEEDS 26TH HADITH OF AN-NAWAWI

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge (IRK)
q: Among the obligatory steps of ablution are intention, washing the face, washing the hands to the elbow and also
explanation: The obligatory steps of ablution (wudu) in Islam include intention (niyyah), washing the face, washing the hands up to the elbows, and washing the feet up to the ankles. Therefore, the correct answer is 'washing the feet'.

explanation: Wiping the head from front to back is part of wudu in Islam.

explanation: In Q.5:7, Allah says, "if ye are ill or on a journey" and ye find no water, then perform At-Tayammum

explanation: There are 7 Takbeerāt in the first Rak'ah of Eid prayer.

topics.title: IBADAT AND THEIR TYPES : SALAH

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge (IRK)
q: An Imam leading prayer, forgetfully rises when he is to sit, he is alerted by saying
explanation: If an Imam leading prayer forgetfully rises when he is to sit, he is alerted by saying 'Subhanallah'. This is based on the Hadith of the Prophet Muhammad (peace be upon him) where he said that if something happens in the prayer, men should say 'Subhanallah' and women should clap their hands. Therefore, the correct option is 'Subhanallah'.

topics.title: IBADAT AND THEIR TYPES : SALAH

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge (IRK)
q: Fasting has been prohibited on doubtful day which is
explanation: Fasting has been prohibited on the 'doubtful day' which is the 30th of Sha'aban. This is because it's uncertain whether it's the last day of Sha'aban or the first day of Ramadan. Therefore, to avoid confusion and possible error in starting the fast of Ramadan, it's recommended not to fast on this day. Hence, the correct option is '30th of Sha'aban'.

topics.title: IBADAT AND THEIR TYPES : SAWM

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge (IRK)
q: Those who are not Pilgrims are advised to fast on the day of
explanation: Those who are not pilgrims are advised to fast on the day of Arafat. The Prophet Muhammad (peace be upon him) said that fasting on the day of Arafat expiates the sins of the previous year and the coming year. Therefore, the correct option is 'Arafat'.

topics.title: IBADAT AND THEIR TYPES : HAJJ

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge (IRK)
q: The permission to marry up to four wives in Islam is aimed at
explanation: It is aimed at preventing adultery and preserving chastity.

topics.title: FAMILY MATTERS : MARRIAGE

q: The Prophet (SAW) said, "Of all things lawful, divorce is the most hateful to Allah" because it exposes the parties to
explanation: Talaq (divorce) may lead to conflicts and family provocation.

explanation: Imam Malik, also known as Malik ibn Anas, was born in Madinah. He was a prominent Islamic scholar during his time and is best known for his work on Islamic jurisprudence (Fiqh) and Hadith.

explanation: Pleasure of Allah could be attained here and thereafter through and thereafter through total adherance to the Shariah.

topics.title: ARTICLE OF FAITH : BELIEF IN ALLAH

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge (IRK)
q: Lending out money with interest in Islam is
explanation: Lending out money with interest in Islam is referred to as 'riba'. Riba, or usury, is prohibited in Islam because it is considered exploitative. According to Islamic law, making money from money, such as charging interest, is not a legitimate, ethical, or fair form of business.

topics.title: ISLAMIC ECONOMIC SYSTEM : ISLAMIC ATTITUDE TO RIBA HADITH 6TH OF AN-NAWAWI

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge (IRK)
q: Zakat is an economic system that deemphasizes
explanation: Zakat is an economic system in Islam that deemphasizes the accumulation of wealth. It is a form of almsgiving to the poor and needy that is religiously obligatory for all who are able to do so. It serves to purify one's wealth, encourage social harmony, and reduce inequality.

topics.title: ISLAMIC ECONOMIC SYSTEM : ISLAMIC SOURCES OF REVENUE: ZAKAH, JIZYAH, KHARAJ AND GHANIMAH

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge (IRK)
q:
"O Allah! Lord of power (and rule), Thou giveth power to whom thou pleaseth, and thou strippest off power from whom thou pleaseth".
The verse above describes Allah"s political power as most
explanation: The verse describes Allah's political power as sovereign. Sovereignty refers to the full right and power of a governing body over itself, without any interference from outside sources or bodies. In the context of the verse, it means that Allah has the ultimate authority to give and take power as He pleases.

topics.title: ARTICLE OF FAITH : BELIEF IN ALLAH

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge (IRK)
q: The core of Muhammad"s (SAW) da'awah in Makkah was on
explanation: The core of Prophet Muhammad's (SAW) da'awah (call to Islam) in Makkah was on the unity of Allah. This is known as Tawhid in Islamic theology, which asserts that Allah is one and unique.

explanation: The Battle of Badr was fought in the 2nd year after Hijrah, which corresponds to 624 A.H. (After Hijrah). Hijrah is the migration of Prophet Muhammad (SAW) from Makkah to Madinah.

explanation: The Jahihyyah Arabs believed in Allah partially but they believed that the idols are intermidiaries (go between) between them and Allah.

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge (IRK)

explanation: Amr B. As was the companion of Prophet Muhammad who introduced Islam to North Africa through Egypt. He was a military commander and statesman, who led the Muslim conquest of Egypt in 640 AD.

explanation: When the prophet was about 40 years of age, he usually visit care Hirah to withdraw himself from vices in the community . At age 40, Angel Jubril appeared to him and gave him the revelation of Quran.

topics.title: THE QUR’AN AND HADITH – REVELATION OF THE GLORIOUS QUR’AN : VISITS OF THE PROPHET (SAW) TO CAVE HIRA

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge (IRK)
q: One of the importance of the first revelation was the
options: description of the Arabian peninsula to the Prophet (SAW)
description of the steps of acquiring knowledge
explanation: One of the importance of the first revelation was the dissemination of knowledge. The first revelation marked the beginning of the prophethood of Muhammad and the start of the spread of Islam, which brought a new set of knowledge and teachings to the people.

topics.title: THE QUR’AN AND HADITH – REVELATION OF THE GLORIOUS QUR’AN : HIS REACTION TO THE FIRST REVELATION AND ITS IMPORTANCE

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge (IRK)
q: The gradual spiritual and moral development of Muslims was the wisdom behind the
explanation: The wisdom behind the gradual spiritual and moral development of Muslims was the piecemeal revelation of the Glorious Qur'an. The Qur'an was not revealed all at once but over a period of 23 years, allowing the Muslims to gradually understand and implement its teachings in their lives.

topics.title: THE QUR'AN AND HADITH-REVELATION OF THE GLORIOUS QUR'AN : PIECEMEAL REVELATION

explanation: Preservation of the Quran is quaranteed by Allah because it was revealed from Him. Q1519

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge (IRK)
q: The battle that served as a pointer to the compilation of the Glorious Qur'an was fought at
explanation: Many Muslims (Qurau) memorizers of the Quran had fallen down at the battle field (yamnmah) in 633. As a result of this, Umar brin khatab suggested to the caliph AbuBakr that Quran should be compiled.

topics.title: THE LIFE OF PROPHET MUHAMMAD (SAW) : THE BATTLES OF BADR, UHUD AND KHANDAQ: CAUSES AND EFFECTS

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge (IRK)
q: The committee that standardized the Glorious Qur'an is made up of
explanation: Uthmah bin Affan appointed a four-man committee for standardization of the Quran. The committee was headed by Zaid bin Thabit. Other members are Abdullah bin Zubari, Sad bin Al-As and Abdu-Ralman bin Harith.

topics.title: PRESERVATION OF THE GLORIOUS QUR’AN : RECORDING, COMPILATION AND STANDARDIZATION OF THE GLORIOUS QUR’AN

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge (IRK)
q: A characteristic common to Makkan chapters is that, they
explanation: A characteristic common to Makkan chapters is that they contain a lot of oaths. The Makkan chapters are the chapters of the Qur'an that were revealed while the Prophet Muhammad was in Makkah. These chapters often contain oaths and emphasize the oneness of God, the resurrection, and the judgment day.

topics.title: PRESERVATION OF THE GLORIOUS QUR’AN : DIFFERENCES BETWEEN MAKKAH AND MADINAN SUWAR

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge (IRK)
q: The victory of the Roman Empire over the Persian Empire after the Persians had defeated the Romans as stated in the Glorious Qur'an (Q. 30: 2-3) is an evidence of the
options: beauty of the words of the Glorious Qur'an
simplicity of the wordings of the Glorious Qur'an
explanation: The victory of the Roman Empire over the Persian Empire after the Persians had defeated the Romans as stated in the Glorious Qur'an (Q. 30: 2-3) is an evidence of the authenticity of the Glorious Qur'an. This event was predicted in the Qur'an before it happened, which proves the authenticity of the Qur'an.

topics.title: PROOF OF THE DIVINE AUTHENTICITY OF THE GLORIOUS QUR'AN : UNIQUENESS OF THE GLORIOUS QUR'AN

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge (IRK)
q: The Qur'an is different from other revealed Books because
options: barren women can be helped through it
both spiritual, moral and social values are found in it
one can use it for protection only
explanation: The Qur'an is different from other revealed Books because both spiritual, moral and social values are found in it. The Qur'an is a comprehensive guide for all aspects of life, including spiritual, moral, and social aspects.

topics.title: IMPORTANCE OF THE GLORIOUS QUR’AN : AS SOURCE OF GUIDANCE IN SPIRITUAL, MORAL, ECONOMIC, POLITICAL AND SOCIO-CULTURAL MATTERS.

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge (IRK)
q: Prophet Muhammad (SAW) did not hand-over the Glorious Qur'an to his companions in an arranged form and in a single written volume because
options: he did not want only the Quraish to inherit the book
the revelation did not come in one piece but at intervals
many tribes would want to take over its distribution
explanation: Prophet Muhammad (SAW) did not hand-over the Glorious Qur'an to his companions in an arranged form and in a single written volume because the revelation did not come in one piece but at intervals. The Qur'an was revealed over a period of 23 years, and it was not compiled into a single book during the lifetime of the Prophet.

topics.title: PRESERVATION OF THE GLORIOUS QUR’AN : RECORDING, COMPILATION AND STANDARDIZATION OF THE GLORIOUS QUR’AN

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge (IRK)
q: The correct meaning of tafsir is the
options: detailed explanation and commentary of the Glorious Qur'an
scientific application of the contents of the Glorious Qur'an to human life
true reflections on the contents of the Glorious Qur'an
practical application of the contents of the Glorious Qur'an
explanation: Tafsir is the Arabic word for exegesis, usually of the Qur'an. An author of a tafsir is a mufassir. Tafsir involves explaining, interpreting, expounding, and elucidating the meanings of the Qur'anic verses. Therefore, the correct meaning of tafsir is the detailed explanation and commentary of the Glorious Qur'an.

topics.title: TAFSIR : IMPORTANCE AND TYPES OF TAFSIR

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge (IRK)
q: Tajwid as one of the science of the Glorious Qur'an is important because
options: it allows for the understanding of history of the Glorious Qur'an
it provides the basis for the deeper understanding of the Glorious Qur'an
it provides the knowledge of correct pronounciation and recitation of the Glorious Qur'an
it increases the ability to recite and communicate in Arabic
explanation: Tajwid is a term in Arabic that means to beautify or improve. In the context of the Qur'an, it refers to the rules governing pronunciation during recitation of the Qur'an. Therefore, Tajwid as one of the science of the Glorious Qur'an is important because it provides the knowledge of correct pronunciation and recitation of the Glorious Qur'an.

explanation: Tafkhim Rau. This occur when letter Rau has Dummah.

topics.title: STUDY OF THE ARABIC TEXT OF THE FOLLOWING SUWAR/AYATS WITH TAJWID : AL-MASAD ((Q.111)

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge (IRK)
q: The only Surah in the Glorious Qur'an which must be recited in every obligatory prayer is
explanation: Surah Al-Fatihah is the first chapter (Surah) of the Qur'an. Its seven verses (ayat) are a prayer for the guidance, lordship, and mercy of Allah. This chapter has an essential role in Islamic prayer (Salat). The primary literal meaning of the expression 'al-Fatihah' is 'The Opener,' which could refer to this Surah being 'the opener of the Book' (Fatihat al-kitab), to its being the first Surah recited in full in every prayer cycle (rakʿah), or to the manner in which it serves as an opening for many functions in everyday Islamic life. Therefore, the only Surah in the Glorious Qur'an which must be recited in every obligatory prayer is al-Fatihah.

topics.title: STUDY OF THE ARABIC TEXT OF THE FOLLOWING SUWAR/AYATS WITH TAJWID : AL-FATIHAH
