Be mindful of God, and He will take care of you. Be mindful of Him, and you shall find Him at your side. If you ask, ask of God. If you need help, seek it from God. Know that if the whole world were to gather together in order to help you, they would not be able to help you except if God had written so. And if the whole world were to gather together in order to harm you, they would not harm you except if God had written so. The pens have been lifted, and the pages are dry.' "

topics.title: HADITH : THE STUDY OF THE ARABIC TEXTS OF THE FOLLOWING AHADITH FROMAN-NAWAWI’S COLLECTION: 1,3,5,6,7,9,10,11,12,13,15,16, 18,19,21,22,25,27,34, AND 41

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge (IRK)
q: Ra'y as a mode of Ijtihād technically means
explanation: Ahl ar-ra'y (Arabic: أهل الرأي‎ or aṣḥāb al-raʾy, advocates of ra'y, 'common sense' or 'rational discretion') were an early Islamic movement advocating the use of reasoning to arrive at legal decisions.

topics.title: SOURCES AND SCHOOLS OF LAW : THE FOUR MAJOR SOURCES THE QUR'AN, SUNNAH, IJMA AND QIYAS

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge (IRK)
q: Hadith No 2 of Al-Nawawi's collections was transmitted by _____ and _____
explanation: Hadith No 2 of Al-Nawawi's collections was transmitted by Bukhari and Muslim. These are two of the most respected collectors of Hadith in Islamic tradition.

topics.title: HADITH : THE STUDY OF THE ARABIC TEXTS OF THE FOLLOWING AHADITH FROMAN-NAWAWI’S COLLECTION: 1,3,5,6,7,9,10,11,12,13,15,16, 18,19,21,22,25,27,34, AND 41

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge (IRK)
q: The following are the lessons of Hadith No. 2 of al - Nawawi's collections except
explanation: The lessons of Hadith No. 2 of al-Nawawi's collections include al-Ihsan (doing what is good), al-Imam (faith), and al-Islam (submission to God's will). Al-Sa'ah (the Hour or Day of Judgment) is not a lesson from this Hadith.

topics.title: HADITH : THE STUDY OF THE ARABIC TEXTS OF THE FOLLOWING AHADITH FROMAN-NAWAWI’S COLLECTION: 1,3,5,6,7,9,10,11,12,13,15,16, 18,19,21,22,25,27,34, AND 41

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge (IRK)
q: Man kana Yu'minu billahi is repeated _____ times in Hadith No. 15 of al-Nawawi's collections
explanation: The phrase 'Man kana Yu'minu billahi' (Whoever believes in Allah) is repeated three times in Hadith No. 15 of al-Nawawi's collections.

topics.title: HADITH : THE STUDY OF THE ARABIC TEXTS OF THE FOLLOWING AHADITH FROMAN-NAWAWI’S COLLECTION: 1,3,5,6,7,9,10,11,12,13,15,16, 18,19,21,22,25,27,34, AND 41

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge (IRK)
q: The compilation of Hadith started at the time of
explanation: The compilation of Hadith started at the time of Abdullahi bn Umar

topics.title: HADITH : HISTORY OF HADITH LITERATURE -COLLECTION OF HADITH FROM THE TIME OF THE PROPHET-SAW TO THE PERIOD OF THE SIX AUTHENTIC COLLECTORS OF HADITH

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge (IRK)
q: The first surah in the arrangement of al-Quran is Suratul.
explanation: The first surah in the arrangement of the Quran is Suratul Alaq.

topics.title: STUDY OF THE ARABIC TEXT OF THE FOLLOWING SUWAR/AYATS WITH TAJWID : AL-FATIHAH

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge (IRK)
q: What is the main teaching of suratul Ikhlas?
explanation: The main teaching of Suratul Ikhlas is Monotheism. This Surah emphasizes the oneness of Allah, which is the core principle of Islam's belief system.

topics.title: STUDY OF THE ARABIC TEXT OF THE FOLLOWING SUWAR/AYATS WITH TAJWID : AL -IKHLAS

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge (IRK)
q: Which of the following factors is responsible for the compilation of al-Quran?
explanation: The death of the memorizers of the Quran was one of the main factors responsible for the compilation of al-Quran. After the Battle of Yamama, where many memorizers died, it was decided to compile the Quran into a single book to preserve it.

topics.title: PRESERVATION OF THE GLORIOUS QUR’AN : RECORDING, COMPILATION AND STANDARDIZATION OF THE GLORIOUS QUR’AN

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge (IRK)
q: Alam yaj'al khaydahum fi tadil is translated as
options: And He sent against them flight of birds
Did He not make their treacherous plans go astray
Have you not seen how your Lord dealt with the companions of elephants
Pray to the Lord and make sacrifices
explanation: A lam yaj'al kaydahum fi tadlil is translated as "Did we not make their treacherous plans go astray"

topics.title: STUDY OF THE ARABIC TEXT OF THE FOLLOWING SUWAR/AYATS WITH TAJWID : AL -FALAQ

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge (IRK)
q: The revelation of al-Quran started in _____ CE.
explanation: The revelation of al-Quran started in 610 CE. This is when Prophet Muhammad received his first revelation from Allah through the angel Gabriel.

topics.title: THE QUR’AN AND HADITH – REVELATION OF THE GLORIOUS QUR’AN : HIS REACTION TO THE FIRST REVELATION AND ITS IMPORTANCE

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge (IRK)
q: The last verse of Suratul Ma'un is
explanation: The last verse of Suratul Ma'un is 'Wayaman unal ma'un'. Suratul Ma'un is the 107th chapter of the Quran and it has 7 verses. The last verse translates to 'So woe to those who pray.'

topics.title: STUDY OF THE ARABIC TEXT OF THE FOLLOWING SUWAR/AYATS WITH TAJWID : AL -MAUN

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge (IRK)
q: Suratul Asr has _____ verses.
explanation: Suratul Asr of the Holy Quran Chapter 103 has 3 verses.

topics.title: STUDY OF THE ARABIC TEXT OF THE FOLLOWING SUWAR/AYATS WITH TAJWID : AL -ASR

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge (IRK)
q: Hadith No. 1 of al-Nawawi's collections teaches the importance of
explanation: Hadith No. 1 of al-Nawawi's collections teaches the importance of intention. This Hadith is reported by Umar ibn Al-Khattab who said: 'I heard the Messenger of Allah, peace be upon him, say: 'Actions are but by intention and every man shall have but that which he intended.'

topics.title: HADITH : THE STUDY OF THE ARABIC TEXTS OF THE FOLLOWING AHADITH FROMAN-NAWAWI’S COLLECTION: 1,3,5,6,7,9,10,11,12,13,15,16, 18,19,21,22,25,27,34, AND 41

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge (IRK)
q: According to Hadith No. 23 of al-Nawawi's collections, cleanliness is said to be _______ of faith.
explanation: According to Hadith No. 23 of al-Nawawi's collections, cleanliness is said to be half of faith. This Hadith is reported by Abu Malik Al-Ash'ari who said: 'The Messenger of Allah, peace be upon him, said: 'Cleanliness is half of faith.'

topics.title: HADITH : THE STUDY OF THE ARABIC TEXTS OF THE FOLLOWING AHADITH FROMAN-NAWAWI’S COLLECTION: 1,3,5,6,7,9,10,11,12,13,15,16, 18,19,21,22,25,27,34, AND 41

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge (IRK)
q: One of the following is not an act of Taharah.
explanation: One of the following is not an act of Toharrah, All except Istinshaq is an act of Taharrah.

topics.title: IBADAT AND THEIR TYPES : TAHARAH, ITS TYPES AND IMPORTANCE AL-ISTINJA'/ISTIJMAR, ALWUDU', AT-TAYAMMUM AND AL-GHUSL HADITH 10 AND 23 OF AN-NAWAWI

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge (IRK)
q: A muslim is advised to say _________ when leaving the toilet.
explanation: In Islam, it is advised to say 'Gufranak' when leaving the toilet. This is a form of supplication which means 'I seek Your forgiveness'.

topics.title: IBADAT AND THEIR TYPES : TAHARAH, ITS TYPES AND IMPORTANCE AL-ISTINJA'/ISTIJMAR, ALWUDU', AT-TAYAMMUM AND AL-GHUSL HADITH 10 AND 23 OF AN-NAWAWI

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge (IRK)
q: What is the position of fulfilment of promises in Islam?
explanation: In Islam, the fulfilment of promises is considered compulsory. It is a moral obligation and a sign of a believer's truthfulness and integrity.

topics.title: MORAL LESSONS IN THE GLORIOUS QUR’AN AND HADITH : TRUST AND OBLIGATIONS (Q:4:58) (Q.5:1) AND PROMISES (Q.16:91) HADITH ‘HE HAS (REALLY) NO FAITH ….. NOT FULFILLED HIS PROMISE” (BAIHAQI)

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge (IRK)
q: Hadith is the _____ source of Islamic law?
explanation: Hadith is the second source of Islamic law, after the Quran. Hadiths are the sayings, actions, and approvals of the Prophet Muhammad (SAW) regarding daily life, and they serve as a source of guidance for Muslims.

topics.title: SOURCES AND SCHOOLS OF LAW : THE FOUR MAJOR SOURCES THE QUR'AN, SUNNAH, IJMA AND QIYAS

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge (IRK)
q: According to Hadith No. 7 of Al- Nawawi's collections, religion is sincerity to the following except
explanation: According to Hadith No. 7, Religion is sincerely to the following except Leaders of the Muslim.

topics.title: HADITH : THE STUDY OF THE ARABIC TEXTS OF THE FOLLOWING AHADITH FROMAN-NAWAWI’S COLLECTION: 1,3,5,6,7,9,10,11,12,13,15,16, 18,19,21,22,25,27,34, AND 41

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge (IRK)
q: Anas who related Hadith No. 13 of al-Nawawi's collections was a __________ to the Prophet (SAW)
explanation: Anas who related Hadith No. 13 was a neighbor to the Prophet (S.A.W).

topics.title: THE LIFE OF PROPHET MUHAMMAD (SAW) : HIS BIRTH AND EARLY LIFE

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge (IRK)
q: Actions shall be judged according to intentions is Hadith ______ of al-Nawawi's collections.
explanation: The Hadith 'Actions shall be judged according to intentions' is the first Hadith in Imam al-Nawawi's collection of forty Hadiths. This Hadith emphasizes the importance of intention in Islam, stating that the value of an action is determined by the intention behind it.

topics.title: HADITH : THE STUDY OF THE ARABIC TEXTS OF THE FOLLOWING AHADITH FROMAN-NAWAWI’S COLLECTION: 1,3,5,6,7,9,10,11,12,13,15,16, 18,19,21,22,25,27,34, AND 41

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge (IRK)
q: Which of the following is not among the revealed books?
explanation: Diwan is not among the revealed books. The revealed books in Islam are the Qur'an, the Injil (Gospel), the Tawrah (Torah), and the Zabur (Psalms). The Diwan is a collection of poems, not a revealed book.

topics.title: ARTICLE OF FAITH : HIS BOOKS

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge (IRK)
q: Hajj combined with Umrah is known as
explanation: Hajj combined with Umrah is known as Qiran. In Qiran, a pilgrim performs both the Umrah and the Hajj together with the same Ihram (state of ritual purity).

topics.title: IBADAT AND THEIR TYPES : HAJJ

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge (IRK)
q: Pilgrims combine salatul ______ and ________ in Muzdalifah.
explanation: Pilgrims combine Salatul Magrib and Isha' in Muzdalifah. Muzdalifah is an open, level area near Mecca where pilgrims spend the night in the open after the day of Arafah during Hajj.

topics.title: IBADAT AND THEIR TYPES : HAJJ

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge (IRK)
q: Khadijah was _________ years old when she married the Prophet (SAW)
explanation: Khadijah was 40 years old when she married the Prophet Muhammad (SAW). She was a successful businesswoman and the first wife of the Prophet.

topics.title: THE LIFE OF PROPHET MUHAMMAD (SAW) : HIS BIRTH AND EARLY LIFE

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge (IRK)
q: The battle of Badr was fought in _______ year AH.
explanation: The Battle of Badr, a key battle in the early days of Islam and a turning point in Prophet Muhammad's struggle with his opponents among the Quraysh in Mecca, took place in the 2nd year of the Hijri calendar (AH).

topics.title: THE LIFE OF PROPHET MUHAMMAD (SAW) : THE BATTLES OF BADR, UHUD AND KHANDAQ: CAUSES AND EFFECTS

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge (IRK)
q: Who among the following was killed at the battle of Badr?
explanation: Hamzah was the companion of the prophet that was killed in the Battle of Badr.

topics.title: THE LIFE OF PROPHET MUHAMMAD (SAW) : THE BATTLES OF BADR, UHUD AND KHANDAQ: CAUSES AND EFFECTS

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge (IRK)
q: A person who joins the Imam before rising from the ruku'. He
options: must not join the Imam at all
explanation: In Islamic prayer (Salat), if a person joins the Imam (the one who leads the prayer) before the Imam rises from the Ruku' (the bowing position), then he is considered to have caught that Raka'ah (unit of prayer).

topics.title: IBADAT AND THEIR TYPES : SALAH

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge (IRK)
q: How many muslims participated in the battle of Badr?
explanation: The Battle of Badr was the first major battle in Islam and it was fought by the Muslims against the Quraysh of Mecca. The number of Muslims who participated in this battle was approximately 313.

topics.title: THE LIFE OF PROPHET MUHAMMAD (SAW) : THE BATTLES OF BADR, UHUD AND KHANDAQ: CAUSES AND EFFECTS

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge (IRK)
q: One of the following is not required in Umrah?
explanation: Umrah is a pilgrimage in Islam, but unlike Hajj, it is not time specific and can be performed at any time of the year. Arafat, which is a key ritual in the Hajj pilgrimage, is not required in Umrah. The rituals of Umrah include the Miqat, Sa'y, and Talbiyyah.

topics.title: IBADAT AND THEIR TYPES : HAJJ

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge (IRK)
q: The fasting of Tasu'a and Ashura are observed in the month of
explanation: The fasting of Tasu'a and Ashura are observed in the month of Muharram. Tasu'a is observed on the 9th day of Muharram and Ashura is observed on the 10th day of Muharram in the Islamic calendar.

topics.title: IBADAT AND THEIR TYPES : SAWM

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge (IRK)
q: Jinns were created out of
explanation: According to Islamic tradition, Jinns were created out of smokeless fire. This is mentioned in the Quran in Surah Al-Rahman (55:15) and Surah Al-Hijr (15:27).

topics.title: ARTICLE OF FAITH : BELIEF IN ALLAH

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge (IRK)
q: Kalimatus-shahadah is the key to enter
explanation: Kalimatus-shahadah, which translates to 'the word of testimony', is the key to enter Islam. It is a declaration of faith and trust that professes that there is only one God (Allah) and Muhammad is God's messenger. It is a set statement normally recited in Arabic: lā ʾilāha ʾillā-llāhu muḥammadun rasūlu-llāh.

topics.title: TAWHID AND FIQH : KALIMATUSH-SHAHADAH

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge (IRK)
q: Which of the following prophets is not among the Ulul-Azm?
explanation: The Ulul-Azm prophets are those messengers who were endowed with determination. According to Islamic tradition, there are five Ulul-Azm prophets: Noah, Abraham, Moses, Jesus, and Muhammad. Therefore, Dawud (AS) is not among the Ulul-Azm.

topics.title: ARTICLE OF FAITH : HIS PROPHETS: ULUL-AZMI

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge (IRK)
q: Hafsah the Prophet's (SAW) wife is a daughter to
explanation: Hafsah, the Wife of Prophet is the daughter of Umar.

topics.title: THE LIFE OF PROPHET MUHAMMAD (SAW) : HIS BIRTH AND EARLY LIFE

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge (IRK)
q: The person who accompanied the prophet (SAW) during Hijrah to Madinah was
explanation: The person who accompanied the Prophet Muhammad (SAW) during his migration (Hijrah) from Mecca to Medina was Abu Bakr. This is a well-known event in Islamic history.

topics.title: THE LIFE OF PROPHET MUHAMMAD (SAW) : THE HIJRAH

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge (IRK)
q: The youngest person to accept Islam was
explanation: The youngest person to accept Islam was Aliyu (Ali ibn Abi Talib). He was raised in the household of the Prophet (SAW) and accepted Islam when he was still a child.

topics.title: THE LIFE OF PROPHET MUHAMMAD (SAW) : HIS BIRTH AND EARLY LIFE

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge (IRK)
q: The prophet (SAW) open da'wah earned him
explanation: The propeht (S.A.W) dawah earned him progress by increasing his rank.

topics.title: THE LIFE OF PROPHET MUHAMMAD (SAW) : DA’WAH IN MAKKAH AND MADINAH

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge (IRK)
q: The birth of the Prophet (SAW) coincided with the attack on ka'bah by
explanation: The birth of the Prophet Muhammad (SAW) coincided with the attack on the Ka'bah by Abrahah, the Christian ruler of Yemen. This event is referred to in Surah Al-Fil (The Elephant) in the Quran.

topics.title: THE LIFE OF PROPHET MUHAMMAD (SAW) : HIS BIRTH AND EARLY LIFE

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge (IRK)
q: Suraful _______ made mention of the graveyard
explanation: Surah Zalzalah (The Earthquake) makes mention of the graveyard. This Surah discusses the Day of Judgment, when the earth will give up its burdens and human deeds will be weighed.

topics.title: STUDY OF THE ARABIC TEXT OF THE FOLLOWING SUWAR/AYATS WITH TAJWID : AL -KAWTHAR

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge (IRK)
q: Which of the following Suwar encourages acquisition of knowledge?
explanation: Surah Alaq is the first revelation given to Prophet Muhammad and it begins with 'Read! In the Name of your Lord Who has created (all that exists)'. This clearly encourages the acquisition of knowledge.

topics.title: STUDY OF THE ARABIC TEXT OF THE FOLLOWING SUWAR/AYATS WITH TAJWID : AL-ALAQ

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge (IRK)
q: According to Hadith 25 of al-Nawawi's collection, the rich people have gone with ______
explanation: According to Hadith 25 of al-Nawawi's collection, the Prophet Muhammad said, 'The wealthy have gone with the rewards'. This implies that the rich people have taken the rewards of the hereafter by using their wealth in the way of Allah.

topics.title: HADITH : THE STUDY OF THE ARABIC TEXTS OF THE FOLLOWING AHADITH FROMAN-NAWAWI’S COLLECTION: 1,3,5,6,7,9,10,11,12,13,15,16, 18,19,21,22,25,27,34, AND 41

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge (IRK)
q: Subhana Rabbiyal' Azim wa bi Hamdihi is recited in
explanation: 'Subhana Rabbiyal' Azim wa bi Hamdihi' is a phrase that is recited in Ruku' (bowing during prayer). It means 'Glory be to my Lord, the Great, and with His praise'.

topics.title: IBADAT AND THEIR TYPES : SALAH

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge (IRK)
q: The narrator of Hadith No. 1 of al-Nawawi's collections is
explanation: Hadith 1 of An Nawawi collection is narrated by Abdullahi bn Umar.

topics.title: HADITH : THE STUDY OF THE ARABIC TEXTS OF THE FOLLOWING AHADITH FROMAN-NAWAWI’S COLLECTION: 1,3,5,6,7,9,10,11,12,13,15,16, 18,19,21,22,25,27,34, AND 41

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge (IRK)
q: Qu'ran is an evidence for or against you is part of Hadith _____ of al-Nawawi's collections.
explanation: Hadith 23 of An-Nawawi collection shows Quran as an evidence.

topics.title: HADITH : THE STUDY OF THE ARABIC TEXTS OF THE FOLLOWING AHADITH FROMAN-NAWAWI’S COLLECTION: 1,3,5,6,7,9,10,11,12,13,15,16, 18,19,21,22,25,27,34, AND 41

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge (IRK)
q: Hadith ____ of al-Nawawi's collections warns against pokenosing.
explanation: Hadith 8 of An-Nawawi collection warns against pokenosing.

topics.title: HADITH : THE STUDY OF THE ARABIC TEXTS OF THE FOLLOWING AHADITH FROMAN-NAWAWI’S COLLECTION: 1,3,5,6,7,9,10,11,12,13,15,16, 18,19,21,22,25,27,34, AND 41

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge (IRK)
q: Imamul Bukhari was born in the year _____ A.H
explanation: Imamul Bukhari was born in the year 194 A.H. Imam Bukhari is a famous Islamic scholar known for his collection of Hadiths, Sahih al-Bukhari, which is considered one of the most authentic collections of Hadiths.

topics.title: HADITH : THE SIX SOUND COLLECTORS OF HADITH – BIOGRAPHIES AND THEIR WORKS.

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge (IRK)
q: "Whoever does any work which is not in the teaching of Islam will be rejected" is found in Hadith _____ of al-Nawawi's collections.
explanation: Hadith ten teaches that whoever brings any work without the teaching of Islam will be rejected.

topics.title: HADITH : THE STUDY OF THE ARABIC TEXTS OF THE FOLLOWING AHADITH FROMAN-NAWAWI’S COLLECTION: 1,3,5,6,7,9,10,11,12,13,15,16, 18,19,21,22,25,27,34, AND 41

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge (IRK)
q: "Umul-Mu'minin" as mentioned in Hadith No. 5 of al-Nawawi's collection refers to
explanation: 'Umul-Mu'minin' as mentioned in Hadith No. 5 of al-Nawawi's collection refers to A'ishah. 'Umul-Mu'minin' translates to 'Mother of the Believers', a title given to the wives of Prophet Muhammad (PBUH). A'ishah was one of the wives of Prophet Muhammad (PBUH).

topics.title: HADITH : THE STUDY OF THE ARABIC TEXTS OF THE FOLLOWING AHADITH FROMAN-NAWAWI’S COLLECTION: 1,3,5,6,7,9,10,11,12,13,15,16, 18,19,21,22,25,27,34, AND 41

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge (IRK)
q: The first person to accept Islam amongst the slaves was ______
explanation: The first person to accept Islam amongst the slaves was Bilal bn Rabah. Bilal was an Ethiopian slave who was known for his beautiful voice with which he called people to their prayers. He was one of the most trusted and loyal companions of Prophet Muhammad (PBUH).

topics.title: THE LIFE OF PROPHET MUHAMMAD (SAW) : HIS CALL TO PROPHETHOOD

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge (IRK)
q: Omission of Tashahhud during prayer should be corrected by _______
explanation: Omittion of Tashahud during prayer should be corrected by Sujudul-Qabli.

topics.title: IBADAT AND THEIR TYPES : SALAH

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge (IRK)
q: Which of the following Ahadith of al-Nawawi's collections discourages selfishness?
explanation: The Thirteenth Hadith of al-Nawawi's collections discourages selfishness. It states that 'None of you truly believes until he loves for his brother what he loves for himself.' This Hadith teaches Muslims to be selfless and considerate of others.

topics.title: HADITH : THE STUDY OF THE ARABIC TEXTS OF THE FOLLOWING AHADITH FROMAN-NAWAWI’S COLLECTION: 1,3,5,6,7,9,10,11,12,13,15,16, 18,19,21,22,25,27,34, AND 41

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge (IRK)
q: The number of Muslims who participated in the battle of Badr are _____ hundred.
explanation: The Battle of Badr, which took place in 624 AD, was one of the most significant battles in Islamic history. The number of Muslims who participated in this battle was approximately three hundred.

topics.title: THE LIFE OF PROPHET MUHAMMAD (SAW) : THE BATTLES OF BADR, UHUD AND KHANDAQ: CAUSES AND EFFECTS

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge (IRK)
q: Which of the following prayers has a complete silent recitation?
explanation: Zuhr has a silent recitation in prayers but the likes of Maghrib, Isha, Subhi has a loud recitation.

topics.title: IBADAT AND THEIR TYPES : SALAH

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge (IRK)
q: The following are exempted from fasting except
explanation: In Islam, certain individuals are exempted from fasting during the month of Ramadan. These include the insane, menstruating women, pregnant women, the elderly, and the sick. However, being rich does not exempt one from fasting. Therefore, a rich man is not exempted from fasting.

topics.title: IBADAT AND THEIR TYPES : SAWM

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge (IRK)
q: Matn is the ________ of the Hadith.
explanation: Matn are the backbone, or the content of the report, so they are reporters.

topics.title: HADITH : AUTHENTICATION OF HADITH

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge (IRK)
q: Khula fa'ul - Rashidun are ______ in number.
explanation: Khulafa al-Rashidun refers to the 'Rightly Guided Caliphs' in Islamic history. They are four in number: Abu Bakr, Umar, Uthman, and Ali.

topics.title: THE RIGHTLY GUIDED CALIPHS AL-KHULAFA'U RASHIDUN : THE LIVES AND CONTRIBUTIONS OF THE FOUR RIGHTLY GUIDED CALIPHS

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge (IRK)
q: Taharah simply means _______
explanation: Taharah in Islamic jurisprudence refers to 'Purification'. It is the state of being physically and ritually clean according to Islamic law.

topics.title: IBADAT AND THEIR TYPES : TAHARAH, ITS TYPES AND IMPORTANCE AL-ISTINJA'/ISTIJMAR, ALWUDU', AT-TAYAMMUM AND AL-GHUSL HADITH 10 AND 23 OF AN-NAWAWI

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge (IRK)
q: Subhana Rabbiyal A'la wa bihamdihi is recited in ________
explanation: Subhana Rabiyyal Aola wabihamdihi is reacted in the bowing part of salaat (Ruk'u).

topics.title: IBADAT AND THEIR TYPES : SALAH

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge (IRK)
q: Ghusluf ______ is performed after the stoppage of the menstrual blood.
explanation: Ghusl is a full-body ritual purification mandatory before the performance of various rituals and prayers. Ghusl al-Hayd is the obligatory bath after the cessation of menstrual blood. Therefore, the correct answer is 'Haydah'.

topics.title: IBADAT AND THEIR TYPES : TAHARAH, ITS TYPES AND IMPORTANCE AL-ISTINJA'/ISTIJMAR, ALWUDU', AT-TAYAMMUM AND AL-GHUSL HADITH 10 AND 23 OF AN-NAWAWI

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge (IRK)
q: In al-Quran 109, Allah (SWT) says: I do not ________ that which you worship.
explanation: In Surah Al-Kafirun (109), Allah (SWT) says: 'I do not worship that which you worship.' This Surah was revealed in Makkah and it has 6 verses. The Surah emphasizes the Islamic concept of Tawheed and negates shirk.

topics.title: STUDY OF THE ARABIC TEXT OF THE FOLLOWING SUWAR/AYATS WITH TAJWID : AL - KAFIRUN

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge (IRK)
q: Lailatul Qadr is found in the month of _______
explanation: Lailatul Qadr, also known as the Night of Power, is found in the month of Ramadan. It is considered the holiest night of the year for Muslims, and is traditionally celebrated on the 27th day of Ramadan. It is the night when the Quran was first revealed to the Prophet Muhammad (SAW).

topics.title: IBADAT AND THEIR TYPES : SAWM

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge (IRK)
q: Fal'ya budu Rabba hadhal Bayt means ________
options: let them worship the Lord of this house
pray to the Lord and make sacrifices
teaches man what he does not know
explanation: 'Fal'ya budu Rabba hadhal Bayt' translates to 'let them worship the Lord of this house.' It is a verse from Surah Al-Quraish (106:3). The 'house' in this context refers to the Kaaba in Makkah.

topics.title: STUDY OF THE ARABIC TEXT OF THE FOLLOWING SUWAR/AYATS WITH TAJWID : AL -MASAD

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge (IRK)

explanation: Suratul Asr is the 103rd Surah of the Quran and it consists of three verses. The Surah takes its name from the word al-Asr in the first verse. This is not only the name but also the title of this Surah, mentioning the period of time.

q: Al-Hakim means The ________
explanation: Al-Hakim is one of the 99 names of Allah (SWT) and it means 'The Wise.' It is used in the Quran in verses 2:129, 2:228, 2:240, 3:18, 4:26, 5:118, 9:94, 9:105, 33:72, 35:28, 40:2, 43:84, 45:2, 46:2, 57:1, 66:2, and 83:9.

topics.title: ARTICLE OF FAITH : BELIEF IN ALLAH

subject: Islamic Religious Knowledge (IRK)
q: Tayammum and wudu are vitiated by the following except _______
explanation: Tayammum and wudu are Islamic rituals of purification. Tayammum is a dry ablution using sand or dust, performed in place of ritual washing (wudu or ghusl) if clean water is not available or if one is suffering from moisture-induced skin inflammation. Wudu is the Islamic procedure for washing parts of the body using water, typically in preparation for formal prayers (salat). The availability of water does not vitiate (invalidate) Tayammum or Wudu, rather it is a requirement for Wudu. The other options such as loss of sense, passing stool, and passing urine are conditions that vitiate Wudu and Tayammum.

explanation: The pilgrims go to Arafat on the 9th day of Dhul-Hijjah. This day is known as the Day of Arafat. It is the second day of the Hajj pilgrimage and the day before Eid al-Adha. Arafat Day is considered one of the most important days, not just of Hajj, but of the Islamic calendar.

explanation: Abu Dawud was born in the province of Sijistan, which is in modern-day Iran. He was a noted collector of hadiths (sayings and actions of the Prophet Muhammad), and his collection, Sunan Abu Dawud, is one of the six canonical collections of Hadiths in Sunni Islam.

explanation: Hadith refers to the sayings, actions, and silent approvals of the Prophet Muhammad (SAW). It is a major source of Islamic law and moral guidance, second only to the Quran. This makes option 3 the correct answer.

explanation: In Islam, a woman is required to cover her entire body when in the presence of men who are not her immediate family. This includes her hair and body, but not her face and palms. Therefore, option 2 is the correct answer.

explanation: In Islamic law, the punishment for theft, under certain conditions, is amputation of the hand. This is based on Quranic verses (5:38) and Hadiths. Therefore, option 4 is the correct answer.

explanation: In Islamic terminology, a munafiq is a hypocrite who outwardly practices Islam while inwardly concealing his disbelief, perhaps even unknowingly. Lack of fulfilling promises is a sign of hypocrisy, thus the correct answer is 'munafiq'.

options: it marks the beginning of Hajj rites
pilgrims visit the Jamarat and make a sacrifice
the first Tawaf is performed at the Ka'bah
pilgrims spend the night in supplication at Muzdalifah
explanation: The 8th of Dhul-Hijjah is known as Yawmut-Tarwiyyah because it marks the beginning of the Hajj rites. On this day, pilgrims begin their journey from Makkah to Mina, marking the start of the Hajj pilgrimage. The term 'Tarwiyyah' refers to the act of quenching thirst, as it was traditionally the day when pilgrims would collect water for their journey.

explanation: A major teaching of the belief in the hereafter is that Muslims will enjoy the intercession of Prophet Muhammad(SAW). This is based on the belief that Prophet Muhammad will intercede on behalf of his followers on the Day of Judgment.

explanation: The first revelation to the Prophet Muhammad (SAW) is in the Qur'an chapter 96, verses 1-5. This chapter is also known as Al-Alaq. The first five verses were revealed to the Prophet Muhammad (SAW) in the cave of Hira.

q: The following are authors of Sihahus-Sitta except
explanation: Sihahus-Sitta refers to the six major Hadith collections in Sunni Islam. These collections were compiled by six authors: Bukhari, Muslim, Abu Dawud, Tirmidhi, Nasai, and Ibn Majah. Malik b. Anas, the founder of the Maliki school of thought, is not one of these six authors. He compiled his own Hadith collection known as the Muwatta, but it is not part of the Sihahus-Sitta.

explanation: In Suratul-Kafirun, the word 'A'bud (worship) and its derivatives occur six times. This Surah emphasizes the Islamic concept of Tawhid (monotheism) and categorically rejects polytheism and disbelief.

Quran 24 vs 31

Who made the statement above?
explanation: This happened when angel Jibril visited the Prophet Muhammad(SAW) in the cave of Hira. He was shocked at what was revealed to him and he went to his wife Khadija. She took the Prophet to her uncle,Waraqah who confirmed that Muhammad would lead the people as a Prophet of Allah and would be persecuted by Makkans.

explanation: The 93rd chapter of the Quran, Surah Duha focuses mainly on the Prophet. This surah was revealed to Prophet Muhammad (saw) at a time when he had not received any revelation for six months, not even in the form of a dream! The Prophet (saw) was in a very disturbed state of mind, feeling negative and depressed and believing that Allah was displeased with him, had forgotten him, and did not want him as a Nabi anymore.

explanation: The expression 'Man kana yu minu billahi wal yawmil akhir fal yaqual khayran aw li yasmut' translates to 'Whoever believes in Allah and the Last Day should either speak good things or remain silent'. This is a teaching from Prophet Muhammad emphasizing the importance of controlling one's speech.
